features.reviewBody: My name is James Frees and I am Very Grateful to the Staff at Belmont. I especially want to thank Nicole, who was Always there for me and Ms. Paula, and Bryan and Reuben as well as my favorite Nurse, Norine and Ella.I did not want to go. The food was delicious 👌.My Doctor, Mr. Leach and Dr. Goldberg were awesome

Freaking awesome team! Erik and Trish are amazing. Caring and knowledge and invested in getting you back to what you want to do and be. Couldn't possibly  imagine a better experience. Wish I had found them earlier for my first injury. Going to start with them for a separate issue now. (did you know you don't need a doctor to order physical therapy?  You can just call and they will hook you up!!!) P.S. If you are a runner and having any issues at all..don't wait. These guys will help you understand the issue and give you the tools you need to fix it!

My review apparently got deleted but ill write this again. ABSOLUTELY, DO NOT GO TO THIS HOSPITAL FOR HELP. YOU WILL COME OUT WORSE THEN WHEN YOU WALKED IN. Thes staff are very rude, there's absolutely no therapy, and you get no treatment here. There was a grown man who walked in on me on three other woman naked and made inappropriate comments and we all made a complaint and they still had the same male staff at the same unit with us. The psychiatrist barely sees you, they change your medicine without telling you. This hospital visit has guaranteed me to not seek help from this place ever again.

YOU WILL NOT RECEIVE PROPER TREATMENT HERE. I was in the waiting room for 36 hours straight, only allowed to sit in one spot. The CRC staff AND the supervisors are dismissive and disrespectful. I was moved to 3 different units while being admitted in the matter of 7 days where I had to see a totally new set of doctors and social workers each time. The hospital is so unorganized and the staff continuously violates HIPAA law. My mental health declined after being there. I felt so invisible and helpless and they set it up so that you cannot have any real advocates. They DO NOT allow ANY visitation due to Covid but I firmly believe it’s because they don’t want people to know what truly goes on behind closed doors. The staff in the security office will smile in your face and make you believe your loved one is in a good place while the patients are all getting ABUSED. I unfortunately couldn’t leave until a doctor agreed. The Patients’ rights that they have written on paper do not match up with what they do. The experience was honestly TRAUMATIC for the lack of a better word. They give you strong sleep meds and don’t offer talk therapy. if you don’t comply they threaten you with keeping you there longer. This hospital is a maze that you can’t get out of unless you are strong minded and unfortunately they take advantage of people with these issues. I am hoping someday there will be a change and more awareness of hospitals like these. Please get better staff and evaluate the way that you run this place! Too many people’s lives are in the hands of this hospital that feels more like a holding cell. Do not settle for this place.

I need you to know that the Doctor could care LESS. He was the worst part of it all. The reviews are correct. if youre looking for medical help seek outpatient because here you will be held in limbo and asked to wait for a Doctor who will throw any prescription at you DAYSSSS LATER without really hearing you out and tending to your REAL needsOn top of that they dont care to inform first timers. I was forced to stay a couple more days because I was given the 72 hours form too late... do not make that mistake. if you want to be out asap sign the form as soon as you can or you will b singing your freedom awayFor the pros, the self reflection factor is good. So is the groups and you also make good friends in the patients, but you can get all that in an outpatient program. So I wouldnt waste my time unless you are homeless.
Exelente
Great place for assistance

I have a loved one currently in there. He called me and I missed his call by a few minutes. I called back and a lady answered. I told her I understood HIPPA but simply asked if she could give him a message that I called so he could call me back. She said very rudely \"No I'm unfortunately too busy to do that ma'am.\" As someone that works in the mental health field, that is awful.. you should never be too busy to let your patients know that they have support on the outside contacting them. Please don't send your loved ones here. They don't apparently have the time to care for them properly.
I've been a patient there, I know someone who still Might be there to this day. I wish I had a way to say, you're an awesome person, I never \"hated\" u, keep going dawg

There are little fiefdoms here and there.  It seems to have improved over the last 10 years I've used this facility.  Recently, the ER clinic left me hanging without updating me on my care/ treatment for several hours.

I was transferred from another hospital and for that reason I believe, was evaluated rather quickly. On a previous stay, I kind of just showed up.... and 11 hours later, I was finally admitted. 11 hours and a bologna sandwichI don't usually complain about hospital food. It's food, within an institution. It's not going to be 5 star. However, without knowing for certain, I'm thinking prisoners get better food than this. Always cold. Always gross. Unless it's a holiday. Then, they actually serve hot food that is very edible. But, like last time, I had to ask the doctor for supplement shakes. Because, I (Like many others), don't do well without some version of food. Seriously. Just serve salads and sandwiches Friends. We'd all be better off. Or don't bring the food up and let it sit for an hour before servingI liked the groups. It's why I picked this hospital a second time around. Some staff, however, were a-holes and make me wonder why we are allowed to express concerns during the first and last groups.... when we do and are reprimanded for it. Also, we are adults and prefer to watch something above the grade of cartoons sometimes. I'm not asking for rated R here. No need for rape scenes and such. But again, WE ARE ADULTSMy social worker, yet again, didn't really social work. I say yet again, because they either seem to do their job well (Brooke Glenn on Close Ops) or they see you on your last day and that's it (Brooke Glenn different unit/Horsham Clinic... same woman). I'm the one who had to call my Psychiatrist's office to schedule an appointment. Said it wasn't something she did. Really? Listen people. Your social worker, is supposed to set up after care for you. Don't listen to or tolerate that crap. And, when they give you a shi**y explanation to cover their tracks, complain. It's the only way WE get heardOh. When I left I stayed in contact with a fellow patient. Who informed me they found mold in the patient water dispenser and had a problem with bed bugsSo, the only reason this place gets any stars at all, is the groups. And, the fact the doctor apologized to me. Never had a doctor make a mistake and fess up to it. Which earns at least 1 star in my book.

I was clinically kidnapped for my insurance here. It took me years of therapy to undo the damage done here. A therapist here told us “I am the devil.” And to be honest, after all the mental manipulation he did I believe him. They lied to my family over the phone, their social workers work for the money grubbing doctors and the professionals will lie to retain your insurance money. DO NOT let your loved ones get placed here. They up your medications to obscene amounts to render you unable to behave so they can reasonably keep you longer. This facility should be CLOSED DOWN. I had to hire a lawyer to get me released.

I'm 62 years old and NovaCare Bethlehem is the absolute best physical therapy office I have ever been to for myself or my children. Erik is outstanding. His knowledge of my condition far surpasses any other therapist. He adjusted my PT daily for extreme nerve pain relief and set me up with a home program as wellKellie at the front office was so welcoming and kind. Trish was also very helpfulI have CRPS/RSD in my right foot and ankle which was made worse due to having emergency abdominal surgery with multiple organ failure and 2 aneurysms, 3 months ago. My CRPS/RSD also spread to my other foot and ankle. I was in the 2 hospitals for almost 6 weeksWhen I came to NovaCare I was very weak and could barely walk with a walker so I also had to use a wheelchair. After 4 weeks (3 x week) Erik had me using only a cane. Because Erik was so good, I postponed my vacation for 2 more weeks of PT. Now I can walk without any assistance.

My wife does her rehab at Novacare Bethlehem. The therapists Eric and Trish that my wife uses are fantastic. The customer service with Kelly is great. Couldn’t be happier with the service here and would highly recommend to anyone who needs to rehab an injuryMy wife started with Eric and his dedication and commitment to improving her condition is what convinced her to keep going. Again, couldn’t be happier with the care she’s getting there.

You wait on hold for 20 minutes to never hear back. I have left numerous emails, calls, and attempts to reach the Mazzoni center. They are highly unprofessional in meeting my needs. Every time I try to call them they take days if not a whole week to get back to me and disregard my medical concerns. I told them back in October about a severe medical need that needed attending and they did not seem concerned. I was told by one of the staff members I would be called back by Valarie, but she never called. She left me an email and I replied and they never got back. Ive been dealing with them for years of going back and forth and they are ill equipped. They tell me to travel 3 hours to be seen by a nurse when I need a specialist.

Please replace this place with better people like the staff at Jefferson Hospital. They care more about detox and mental health! This place is a disgrace and waste of people's time when they are trying to save their family members! Philly deserves better!! When a person walks in on their own they are turned away! When family members do a 302 the doctors are to lazy to help! I'm not talking about once or twice!! It's no surprise so many people overdose in Philly!  💔🤔😡

What can I say about a shelter if you've never been to a shelter it's worse than you can imagine if you've been to a shelter this is a little better than the others

FROM THE BOTTOM OF MY HEART. IF YOU HAVE COMPASSION AND CARE FOR YOUR LOVED ONES. DO NOT... I REPEAT DO NOT SEND THEM TO THIS PLACE. IF I COULD GIVE THIS PLACE 0 STARS I WOULDI admitted myself at this location last week. I waited in that waiting room for 18 hours just to get a bed. The waiting room was truly purgatory and seen and heard terrible things. Once I got my room I felt more relaxed at ease and comfortable.  Food was served and food was edible. Bed was okay, but where I truly seen the horrible experience was the staff to patient relationship. Staff straight up mistreated some patients and the enviorment was not safe what so ever. I will be openly saying names of patients and if I could name staff I would but they didn't want to disclose their names prior to being discharged. There was a 31 yo patient that was definately on the slower spectrum. His name is Giovanni. Gio was an innocent person who can't form sentences, can't write his name, and even forgets name quickly. I grew a friendship with him very quickly because of how comfortable he felt coming to me and talking to me about just anything. During my stay he got physically abused by another patient named Marcel. I do not blame Marcel for his actions for he is troubled as well. What my problem was the staff. There was one gentlement that worked there with long dreads and about 5'8 and wore Jordan retro 6s. He didn't want to disclose his name to me at all. He would openly talk bad about the patients, said that they stank, and didn't show hospitality to the patients. Giovanni wanted to use the bathroom and he had the audacity to say that Gio always needs to use the bathroom in a very negative tone. Where it ticked me off was the last day I was there. I had 1 hour before being discharged and Marcel started doing threatening gestures to giovanni. Like I said my problem isn't with Marcel as I said he is troubled himself. This nurse was joking with Marcel in sign language about giovanni and didn't take the threats  that Marcel was doing seriously. I confronted this nurse about the situation and he replied to me \"I don't care!\" I replied to his response with \"why do you work here?\" He obviously had nothing to say and I told the nurse to grow compassion for the patients. He gave the look that he truly didn't care at all. I just want to share this because nobody deserved to be mistreated at this place regardless of their mental state. So please.. never send someone here.
Keep doing what you do 😊😊😊😊 …
Nightmare!  Very hard time getting billing settled.  Never once received an actual hardcopy bill.  GET EVERYTHING IN WRITING!!!  You will be lied to!

Almost a year goes by (the normal amount of time between appointments that I’d had with my old doctor after 3+years of HRT). Ran out of meds, needed a refill and was told I needed to make my next appointment. The morning of, I learned the bus company I normally used for transportation to Philly went under, so I had to reschedule. Doctor wouldn’t refill my scripts unless I got external bloodwork done, citing my safety. Two weeks later, after a few messages trying to get a blood test apt set up elsewhere, no response during that time. Then a week later I get a message saying she hasn’t been in the office and will send a 2 week script to last til my apt. My question, if my health is so damn important that you can’t send one more month, then why would you make me stop them for essentially three weeks only to send a small script?? Now when I go in to my rescheduled apt my labs are obviously gonna be f*d due to your lack of availability.

Hace como 3 meses aquí en el albergue dirigido por Resources For Human Development (RHD por sus siglas en inglés) nos hicieron una reunión de emergencia dónde tuvimos que tomar nuestras pertenencias básicas y salir corriendo (literalmente) de el edificio en dónde estábamos llamado Fernwood. Este Supervisor Regional nos informó que teníamos que abandonar el edificio INMEDIATAMENTE. Este caballero nos dijo y cito sus palabras : \"Yo no dejaría a mis hijos estar ni un solo día aquí\". Cuando le pregunté el motivo del porqué, me ignoro y cambio el tema. Nos mudaron para un comedor adyacente al complejo (que una vez fue un hogar de ancianos) y nos pusieron todos juntos como sardinas en este comedor. Yo soy bien observador y veo que a los siguientes días vino un equipo de demolición a trabajar en ese edificio del que nos sacaron. Pues resulta que el lugar estaba contaminado con black mold y quién sabe que más. Pues saben qué? En el \"nuevo\" edificio también lo hay y tengo fotos. No tan solo lo hay si no que lo trataron de tapar pintando de blanco sobre las superficies, especialmente por donde están los respiradores del aire acondicionado. Osea tapando el problema no resolviendoloPues ahora resulta que hicieron unas pruebas de Covid y alrededor de 23 personas incluyendo mujeres dimos positivo al mismo. Nuevamente vinieron corriendo y nos movieron a un edificio donde \"compartimos\" el baño con mujeres. De que si necesito hacer mis necesidades tengo que tocar la puerta y si hay una dama esperar a qué ella termine para ENTONCES yo hacer mis necesidades si es que no me las he hecho encima esperando. No hay NADIE tomando el tiempo y regulando que sea justo para todos. No hay fuente de agua limpia para beber y TODAS las duchas solo tienen agua fría (la temperatura afuera está a 44° osea se pueden imaginar cómo sale de fría). Solo las bañeras tiene agua caliente pero no tienen ducha. Ayer me bañé con un vasito. Y está quien no se baña porque no quiere (y/o no puede por condiciones de salud) o son impedidos, pero está quien no le importa. La comida nos la traen fría y una cantidad que da vergüenza siendo esto una entidad supervisada y controlada por la ciudad de Philadelphia. Nos tienen en cuarentena 4 personas dentro de una misma habitación. Y honestamente Yo ni se al 100% si estoy positivo o no pues no me dieron mis resultados. Solo vinieron alarmados con una prisa extrema para que agarraramos lo que \"necesitamos\" por 5 días que dura la cuarentena (aparentemente). Aaaaah! Pero si doy positivo en 5 dias me quedo aquí 5 días más. Pero me come por dentro esa seguridad de que me dicen que me voy en 5 días. Y adivinen qué? En vez de hacer pruebas mandatorias a todo el mundo inmediatamente ya le informaron al resto de los residentes que se harán dichas pruebas...el Lunes. Pq el Lunes? Pq si las hacen hoy no hay espacio suficiente en el edificio para tanta gente que Yo estoy súper seguro están positivasQue cosas no???🙄🤔🤦🏻‍♂️🤷🏻‍♂️😠Entre varias irregularidades más. Staffs power tripping, seguridad guillaos de Policías y faltase personal, poca limpieza e higiene entre otras muchas cosasPero así mismo es uno de los albergues menos malos en la ciudad. No sé que hacen con los fondos por parte de quienes se suponen los provean. 🤔

Hello. I am a 13 year old, who has been dealing with mental health issues and mental illness for about 2-3 years so far. I have been to 3 mental facilities, with this one included, and it was by-far the worst one i’ve seen. I had stayed here for around two weeks, and it was easily one of the most traumatizing experiences i’ve had overall in hospitals. And thats saying a lot, considering a good amount of my trauma stems from hospitalization. Anyways, onto the parts of it that make it bad, the staff. There were two staff members i liked. Two. And not even loved, just… liked. There was one staff member, who i will not be naming along with all the others, as i don’t wish to do such, who had literally ripped the head off of a 13 year old girl’s stuffed animal. Ripped it off. All because she was kissing some other girl. First of all, that’s, at the very least to my knowledge, illegal, and second off, morally really bad. There was another staff member, who i am so very tempted to name simply because she was honestly the worst. She would yell and abuse her power as a nurse to get her way, and then say it’s just her personality. She was not fit to be working in a mental hospital, and i stand by that. I would quite honestly rather go through what i was going through when i was first entered into a hospital again, without any support or therapy, than go back to this hospital. Overall, if your kid, or you, cannot deal with loud noises, fighting, yelling, extreme stress, and things such as this, I would highly recommend not going here.

Oh where do I begin. Let’s just say I went in last October and I was really disoriented and was not myself so that weird waiting room was like a breeze. Only because I pleaded for my release after I was harmed by another patient I won’t mention his name my face was horrible. I spoke to2 psychiatrist 1 a woman and the second a male I made him promise to release me. When I was released my husbands wedding band went missing and cash as well. I was paranoid upon entering so I kept all of my jewelry and iPhones iwatch cash etc in the safe. For some reason they opened it laughed showed me. Catalogued my items I saw my wedding bands all 3 but my cash was completely gone and that’s why I went in there in the first place because I was doing things I normally wouldn’t do so to stop spending I voluntarily checked myself in thinking and believing I was in good hands. That was a complete understatement. I suffered. I was told to come back 6 months after treatment to seek a job there. And I’m tempted to because I want the pati To be treated respectfully and with dignity. I have seen a lot enough to have that place shut down but I don’t because most are seeking shelter and food and a connection to other patients. I get it. But it’s a facility where no one should ever feel they want to run away from because it’s f the lack of care. I was in my own world. I later found out I was dehydrated and my blood count was low and blood pressure was uneven that’s why I felt the way I felt in the first place. Why I just didn’t go to the hospital I don’t know why. I would love for this place to be inspected properly and to hire people with actual medical experience so just in case someone goes unconscious they know what to do.

I've had a problem with both my therapist, & psychiatrist..I had to reschedule an appointment with my therapist twice..The first time he called me back to reschedule, the 2nd time I called my therapist 3 different times, on 3 different days, & left 3 messages to reschedule my appointment..I haven't gotten a call back..As for the psychiatrist, I never even got to see him/her..My therapist told me I had to wait until I seen him a few times to make an appointment with the psychiatrist..After about a month & a half of seeing my therapist on a weekly basis I asked him about making an appointment with the psychiatrist, & his response was \"Oh you haven't made an appointment with the psychiatrist yet?...\" So I went to make an appointment with the psychiatrist, & it was over a month away for the closest appointment..My appointment was written on the card as August 1st, so I went on August 1st..Not only was the psychiatrist not there anyway, but supposedly there must have been a \"mistake\" in the system..The computer had my psychiatrists appointment as August 8th..I even showed the girl my card not that it mattered since both of the psychiatrists left early that day anyway..So I get a call today August 8th saying my psychiatrist appointment time is being moved from 1 to 2pm., because the psychiatrist is running late today..Thank God I mentioned to the receptionist that I haven't seen my therapist in a few weeks, because I haven't gotten a call back to make an appointment..If I didn't say that then I wouldn't have found out that I can't even be seen by the psychiatrist today since I haven't seen my therapist..This has to be one of the most careless, unprofessional, unorganized office's I've ever been to!!

THIS IS A REAL POST & NOT A FAKE 5 STAR POSTED BY EMPLOYEES! Worse VA I have ever been at. I even through the years ask doctors why they put up with nasty staff & they all said, do you have any idea how hard it's to fire a VA employee no matter how bad they are! Compared to a VA like Coatesville there is no comparison because that VA treats Vets great & dose all they can do for a Vet & want to do it. Unlike the Philly VA medical center it's like you're bothering them. Plus you can Never personally talk to your doctor there, other than actually going in person to see the doctor there is no emailing them on myheathvet either. I could go on but it's pointless as this has been going on for 20 years there at the Philly VA hospital & it ain't changing, other then words that go nowhere& do nothing! You can kick a dead horse all day long and it is not getting up, it's dead! Note, don't get me wrong there are some good there too but the bad just over shadows the good after 20 years of crap!

Review of my Philadelphia VA Hospital experienceI am A traveling vet heading to Newark, NJ and then on to Philadelphia, PA. When I realized I had left my insulin at one of my stops along the way. I contacted the Philadelphia VA hospital asking if they could give me enough insulin to get back to my home in Michigan. Their reply was very positive and advised me that I would be accommodated.  My immediate impression of the facility upon arrival was very positive. The first person I interacted with couldn't have been more pleasant. She directed me to the emergency room where I was immediately registered as a traveling vet and escorted into a room for vitals and to await my visit with a doctor.  Both the triage nurse and the doctor were pleasant and accommodated my needs in a timely fashion. I was supplied with authorization to receive my insulin from the pharmacy. I then proceeded to pharmacy and was greeted by a friendly pharmacy tech who quickly expedited my prescription. In total, the entire time spent at the hospital spanned a whopping 30 minutes. In summary, I would Rate my experience with full stars and based on my experience, would recommend the Philadelphia VA to any traveling vet with similar needs as mine. Thank you, thank you thank you dr's and staff of the Philadelphia VA for making my experience so pleasant.

I was in there for 7 days. It takes on average 23 hours in the waiting room. They do not take voluntary seriously and manipulate you into not sending a 72 hour notice to leave. The staff stole my credit and debit cards and spent $800 on it. Spoke to the patient advocate and did not help after waiting 7 days for them to see me. Staff encouraged mental health patients to beat each other up (one was sent to the hospital and a staff said “they deserved it”). They would get so rude about asking to unlock the bathroom and made me wait to the point of soiling. I only got to see the psychiatrist for 2 min a day and she made a mistake and forced me to stay an extra day. Your lucky if you get group therapy (no individual therapy) and they rarely take you outside. I spent 5 days without breathing fresh air. My room had a broken heater and they refused to fix it and I had to wear my winter coat to sleep. Male staff will walk on you naked in the shower. You will sit there for 12 hours a day watching the. You have to fight each other for food and half the time the bread was moldy and the food was cold. They rarely refill the tiny water pitcher shared by 24 patients. The staff would complain they were understaffed but was on there phones 99% of the time. they repeatedly threatened and enraged patients to the point people would openly cry and scream to get help from anyone. We were told we were supposed to feel uncomfortable. It was a living nightmare. They don’t see you as people and won’t listen to a word you say. Medication was never given out in time. They ignored patients complaints about there physical health to the point of collapse. They don’t provide interpreters and those who don’t speak English are often discharged after several days without receiving any care. I am now discharged and am suffering from nightmares and resurfaced ptsd symptoms. I watched them refuse to treat a patient once they were admitted because they didn’t take there insurance and they couldn’t get money out of themThis place was horrible and I cried myself to sleep every night in that place. Please don’t send your family and friends there. If you are considering this place for yourself, you can find so much better and you would deserve so much betterI am pressing charges

Wonderful group of people to say the least. Iam grateful to be part of such an amazing team ,who started helping me out about four years ago . They help you with any paperwork, doctors visits, and rides to where ever you need to go. But the greatest part of it all is that they find you housing that you can call home. I love every bit of it to. I'm only a two minute walk to there office in Olney. And Only two blocks from the Septa Subway. They come and  make home visits every two weeks or so, to just check up , which usually is only for about fifteen minutes or so. So not so bad . If you have any questions about anything just ask Pathways . They most of the time have the answers you might be looking for . So if You are struggling , and are homeless, just ask around about Pathways To Housing. Talk to Philadelphia Outreach members  . They can definitely help. That's how I got started  in the program .Hopefully this message and review helps someone along the way.

Erik and Trish remained calm and professional during this time. They both made sure that I had everything that I needed to start feeling better and told me to take as much time as I needed to get better. Their calm and caring mannerism kept me calm during this incident. The minute you meet Erik and Trish you are treated like family. The day of my incident they would not let me leave until I felt better and they promised to call and check up on me in 15 minutes to make sure that I arrived home safely. When Trish called me it just made me feel so cared for by her and Eric and not just another patient they see a few times a week. I will absolutely be referring this team to anyone that may need physical therapy.

Please for the love of god, skip over Mazzoni. Our healthcare system is trash, I know, but I would rather do the full runaround in the cishet world than ever deal with the negligent and careless service I received hereI was a Medicaid patient almost 7 years ago I think and when my Medicaid ran out, I stopped being a patient because i couldn’t afford it, hence the Medicaid in the first place. At the end of LAST YEAR (2021), I received a bill from a collections agency saying that Mazzoni had filed a claim that I owed them. In 2020. Close to a literal decade after being a patientI live in Jersey now and when I had my first appointment with my new primary care doctor, I got an STI screening. Upon receiving my results, both my doctor and myself were confused by a positive test. After some research, conversations, and more doctors appointments, I found out that Mazzoni gave me an “incomplete” round of treatment for a particular STI, according to the employee at the department of health who called me to go over my options. The stigma behind STIs is already heavy enough without an establishment like Mazzoni making literally everything worse by taking shortcuts, and Philadelphia’s queer community suffers for it.

We heard such great things about Rogers OCD treatment program but we had a very negative experienceThe good: While there was a long wait list to get in, the scheduler was very gracious with us. Our teen was very reluctant to work on her OCD and there were a couple of times we had to be bumped down on the waitlist because she refused to go. After about a year between talking with her psychologist and us she seemed ready to truly start addressing her OCDThe bad: Once we were in to the program our teen lied on the intake questionnaire so we were never able to get her the help she needed. She presented herself as not having OCD. That’s not the staffs fault but what happened next really disturbed my wife and I. Some of our teens OCD thoughts, and they are many, seem to center around sexuality. When we talked about this with the counselors and we were made to feel like “the bad guys” for bringing that up. It came across like the Rogers clinicians felt like they needed to stand up for our child against us. Her personal psychiatrist and psychologist, whom I would consider very affirming had said she’s using this as a way to not deal with her OCD. Knowing OCD and how sexuality can be a common theme how does anyone who suffers from this get help from Rogers when your counselors let their biases get in the way?One comment that stood out from a psychiatrist at a prominent PHP program elsewhere was “I'm surprised when a child comes in and identifies as straight now”. Gay people are not mentally ill but wouldn’t more mental health professionals start to wonder “Why are all the teens that need mental health help identifying on the LGBT spectrum?” It’s exploded among our teens friends, parents and society now are very affirming. For some it seems to be a way to fit in, for others it is a symptom of their greater mental health strugglesThe final insult was that the Rogers therapists wanted to refer our teen to a gender clinic. If they would have listened honestly to us we have seen a pattern of this kind of behavior from our teen, masking her OCD with other issues and using these identities as a way to fit in with one friend group when she didn’t with anotherOn top of all that with many very liberal European nations like Sweden, the UK, Norway, France and from across the globe in New Zealand and Australia backing away from adolescent surgeries and pharmaceutical therapies why on earth would you think I would ever subject my child to that?

I had to have my ex-girlfriend 302'd because she suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and was off her meds for a week. Without her medicine she can be an extreme danger to herself and loved ones due to delusional thoughts that over power her mind. I had to have the police bring her to the Crisis Center. I could not have been any happier with the treatment I received there. The nursing staff and front desk woman could clearly see I was in great emotional distress over her well-being. They reassured of her safety now that the ER had her. They showed great empathy and professionalism. I could tell these people genuinely care about the well-being of the patients here. If you are deciding this location from reviews every hospital gets more negative than positive reviews usually by people with a biased agenda or people who can't comprehend triage and think wait times are due to peopel twidling there thumbs. These people don't understand the demand for care for exceeds the availability of care and these people work extremely long and exhausting hours to get everyone treated as effectively and efficiently as possible. Take it from this review which is completely unbiased, this is where you want to be. There long-term treatment center is the highest rated in the keystone area as per Google reviews. I feel much better knowing my ex-lover is in a safe environment with Medical Doctors and Nurses giving her the care she needs. I just need to make sure when she is released we can set up long-term out-patient care for her. But thank you Friends Hospital for being just that, a friend.

It truly is a stepping stone that paves the way to success for those who have a goal in mind.

State regulations state that facilities that medically detox women within child bearing years MUST test them before starting detox and educate them on their options and what would be best. They NEVER told me I was pregnant. They medically detoxed me with subutex and didn't test to see if I was pregnant. They were also just starting the renovations to the detox area while I was there (March 2015) and someone asked a tech how they can afford this after just building the resident houses, the techs reply was \"the heroin epidemic, we are rolling in money from you guys\".... As true as that may be, its a disgusting thing to say to a patient in your facility. Now I've been an addict for 5 years before coming here and had been in and out of rehabs but this was the first time I was so disgusted with a place. I clearly know I have a problem, they told me after being reviewed and talked to, they didn't feel I needed long term treatment and that a 5 day detox was good enough! I looked at my councelor in disgust and told her no I can't be sent back out their I'm telling you I will pick up again I need longer treatment I have a disease! You don't know a good meal until after you've start eating, and you don't know good therapy until after you've healed.  I didn't get needed surgery on my shoulder until five months after an injury, meaning there was nearly four months where all the adjacent muscles atrophied.  Erik and his crew immediately started with techniques (prior to surgery) to minimize further damage and to prep me for the surgery.  After surgery care was great with a good variety of techniques, and when insurance ceased treatments, NovaCare made sure I was supplied with exercises and stretches to continue my treatment after I left.  While the surgery fixed the problem, I credit a large part of my functionality to the friendly and more-than-great therapists at NovaCare.

1 Star:  Great if need to meet court requirement and quit later without incurring high costFinancials: you sign up and pay a co-payment.  If you are in group and one on one therapy, these expenses add up.  What most people do not realize is that the program lasts approximately 18-24 months.  Collectively you are agreeing to start a program that will indefinitely cost you THOUSANDS of $Further Lack of Clarity:  There seems to be various forms about disclosures (operations requirement), but no specifics, outline or syllabus for the program, leaving things vague.  Families are lost without directionLack of Policy:  There seems to be unclear policies at this office that change like the windLack of Support for Family: There is no support for significant others or families.  This seems strange given the reason one would start the program in the first place.  As mentioned, this is only good for the courtsMissing the Mark:  I was told if I had concerns as a spouse, I could contact someone who was not my spouses therapist  and they would relay a message.  This is clearly a tone deft practice that ignores the victim.  The worst thing you could ask someone to do is repeat themselves to strangers.  No support services were offered to me as a spouse and victim of domestic violence.  This further confirms to the victim, there is 100% nothing they can do and creates hopelessnessLicensing & Lack of True Professionals:  IF you look on the website, please note how many “LSW, social workers” appear on the website under staff.  Seeing how dangerous domestic violence is and how other mental health issues can go hand in hand, I do not believe they can direct anyone to anything else other than “talk” therapy.  No mental health evaluations are conducted.  No other treatment is referred or provided such a medicationLack of References:  No references were ever provided.  It would be nice to hear from families or past participants how the program has benefited them.  Then again, if there wasn’t any, of course this wouldn’t be offeredProgress Made in OVER 6 MONTHS:  Participants will learn why they do the things they do which seems great and exciting at first.  However, this will avail them opportunities to provide excuses to their partnersSurvey: Participants are asked to complete a survey.  I’m sorry is this not a business and are you not providing a service?  I encourage all past recipients and their spouses to write PUBLIC reviews.  They likely won’t, because it’s embarrassing to do so.  Keeping the reviews “in house” doesn’t help anyoneHusbands therapist, WHEN we worked with him, was an amazing guy limited by the lack of frame work of the program.  Issue is truly the programs lack of training of their staff and lack of organizationAdditionally,  domestic abusers tend to manipulate and lie.  Without involvement from victims, this program can go on forever without ever addressing issues.  In turn, this process can destroy familiesPlease keep in mind that few programs like this exist across the country.  The reason for this is because domestic violence rehabilitation has low success rates and is not clinically proven to stop behaviorAttend at your own riskThe Courdea website in comparison to results, feels like a diet pill infomercial that prays upon people’s deepest desires…. But hey, I hope you are the next before and after middle aged pant size dropping mom or dad.

My young teenage son, whose emotional maturity is younger, has been at your facility for a week and in that entire time, the psychiatrist I was told that was assigned to my son, Dr. Salaam, hasn't bothered to call me at all.  Not to discuss what brought him there, his history, or even his meds.  I've asked a couple of people to please tell the psychiatrist I would appreciate a call.  In my last conversation with my son's caseworker, who didn't really have a whole lot to say, especially when my son refused to participate in our first family session, I asked her if the psychiatrist has even evaluated my son, had diagnosed him (since so many psychiatrists come up with different diagnoses even though some of the core ones remain), if he had any recommendations yet.  She told me he had listed a  few diagnoses, none of which included the core diagnoses my son has had since he was still a toddler.  He has significant history of trauma including developmental and PTSD, yet no trauma diagnoses was made even though I gave ample history to the weekend person who called me the day after my son's arrival for his history.  I was impressed with your hospital and staff upon arrival and the one and only time I got to speak to my son for a few minutes he mentioned that he was being treated well and the food was OK.  But not hearing from the psychiatrist within my son's first week there is unconscionable and causing me to question my child's care there.  Is this standard procedure at Friends?  I have two children with significant trauma, mental, and behavioral health challenges that  have been in behavioral health hospitals many times and I've always received a call from the psychiatrists within 1 to 3 days after admission.   As a parent, I'm frustrated and disappointed.  It's never easy to send your child to one of these places and you count on professionals to work with you to support your child and you.

I want to believe that this organization does good things for the community, but I haven't seen it, and my personal struggle to deal with them has lead me to have a severely negative opinionI have a niece, a brown agender queer person, who was in need of therapy. Around a year ago I called this place on recommendation, and went through hell trying to get someone from the counseling department to return my calls. No one *ever* answered, I left voicemails, and would wait 3-5 days and get no callback, so I would call againI finally complained to the director of the center and got a callback, and the person who called me was severely indignant and had a terrible attitude, and then gaslit me by trying to suggest *I* was having a terrible attitude and exhibiting \"privilege\" by not allowing them to bully me on the phone for being upset with their terrible, unprofessional behaviorIt's now a year later, and my niece's therapist at another place is moving on, so I wanted to give this place another try, but yet again I am calling and leaving messages that are not being returnedIf I am to believe this organization takes the mental health of young LGBTQIA+ people seriously, I would need to know that they can do something as simple as return a phone call regarding a child's mental health in a reasonable and professional time frameSadly I cannot recommend Attic Youth Center as a positive place for the purpose they claim to serve.

I can not comfortably give this establishment ANY stars. It was a literal nightmare having my daughter in their care. From the beginning, the intake doctor was understanding and knew the severity of my daughter's mental health issues. My daughter was admitted on Dec. 5th. It was stated she had been off her medication for Schizophrenia for 5 months. She was 25 weeks pregnant upon admission. They admitted my daughter and did pretty much nothing for her besides watch her and feed her. They did NOT restart her anti psychotic medication for 8 days. I had to hear and watch my daughter's mental state deteriorate under their care. If you try to get a doctor on the phone with this place, you can literally FORGET IT. My mother and I constantly called and left messages with several workers on her unit who said the doctor would return our calls. We NEVER spoke with any doctors for the almost two weeks she was there. Prior to admittance, my daughter had already signed off on proper permissions to allow the doctors to communicate with my mother and I about her.  The social worker (Allison) is highly unprofessional and lacked empathy for my daughter. She offered unsolicited advice, telling my mother and I that \" She should have consulted with her ob/gyn prior to becoming pregnant about her medications\". My daughter is a mentally ill, 25 year old who doesnt have the same thought process of a woman without mental health issues so for a \"Social Worker,\" working in a profession where you are supposed to be helping and advocating for people who can not articulate for themselves is highly strange and unusual. The lack of professionalism in that remark was uncouth for a person in this field and I am appalled that she felt her judgement would change the severity of my daughter's situation. I would have respected the hospital more if they would have opted to turn away my pregnant daughter so that I could continue to search for a place who would not just \"baby sit and feed\" her but also treat the actual mental illness to get her back well. My daughter was at this facility for 8 DAYS before they finally decided to treat her psychosis. Im obliged to think they took her in to run up her insurance and do the very bare minimum for treatment and thats exactly what happened. Aside from the doctors  being completely unavailable for 13 days to discuss treatment for my daughter,  upon discharge the attending doctor prescribed my daughter a low dose of Paliperodone in the 3mg tab. They prescribed 15 tabs that needed to be cut in half and taken at bedtime. The tabs came un-scored down the middle. The pharmacy was unwilling to split the medication for my daughter and after further investigation I learned that Paliperodone is a slow releasing medication that is NOT INTENDED TO BE SPLIT and can cause severe side effects if cut. Instead of prescribing the exact pills that she took for her last 4 days in the hospital, the doctor prescribed a bigger dosage. After calling back to see if the doctor could rewrite the script to reflect 1.5 mg, dispense 30, to no avail, my daughter was in danger of missing her medication. The Charge Nurse wasnt any better as her phone etiquette reflected agitation with my mother after many days of our concerns being brushed off and no return calls from the attending doctors.  The treatment that my daughter received is indicative of the level of care they are NOT capable of delivering. This was merely an expensive bed and breakfast stay for my daughter.  A staff member was witnessed not using gloves when serving the patients food which is disgusting and unsanitary. If this hospital is afraid to treat pregnant women due to liability then maybe its a good idea to not take them into your facility from the beginning for purposes of running up their insurance. I will be filing every kind of grievance against this facility and the doctors who gave my daughter mediocre care. I am giving them 1 star because its the lowest rating I can give them on google but they deserve 0 star.  Very ghetto, HIGHLY DO NOT RECOMMEND!

My husband had just returned from service and in the VA computer system his age was 63 (mind you he’s 23) so there was very little he could do, (he had severe back pain, and walking too much took a huge toll) we would go see his doctor and she would’nt see him because of the age malifuntion and she would send us to registration which is on the first floor and we would get down there to find them gone for “lunch” for 2-3 hours, or one time we went down and the woman was I kid you not asleep at her desk. This is a well meaning institution with the capability to do a lot of good, but the amount of good it can do is severely undermined by the behavior of specific staff members, most notably Ms. Denise. She has a reputation with the residents of going out of her way to provoke discharges and causing needless antagonism, to the point of causing an autistic resident to have an extreme panic attack. She escalated this panic attack by being physically and verbally aggressive and discharged the resident for panicking, calling in paramedics to hospitalize and conduct a psych evaluation on the mentally ill adolescent whose breakdown she escalated—seemingly deliberately. The shelter’s mission of providing help to disadvantaged and homeless minority youth is not achievable with a staff member like this in their employ, as any good they do could just as easily be undone. She is at best, ableist, and at worst, malicious. Residents should never fear being made homeless again because of antagonistic staff members, and as such I cannot give a positive review.

This is a very well ran facility. I stayed here for 38 days. I am thoroughly impressed by the staff. The accommodations are very supportive of veterans. I stayed in building 39B. There are washer and dryers for your clothes, and plenty of room in an armour for your clothes. I would like to say, the LCSW's Tom & Carol were one of the most down to earth, understanding individuals who you can possibly meet. The health technians- particularly Dennis & Jeff are great guys, who genuinely care about veterans sincerelyThe nurses are PHENOMENAL. Mary, as well as Zedra are fantastic examples of what good people are. The TRAINING and tools I received here cannot be appreciated enough. I am heavily thankful for all the staff at this facility. If you are contemplating coming here, I strongly recommend thatThe patients come from all demographics... If you do not have thick skin, this might not be your cup of tea. (Most veterans do not have this issue anyway.) But as a precaution, some veterans are a bit \"sketchy\". As a generalization, this should not be a deterrent. I met some very good men here, and I am not ashamed to be a fellow veteran in the same program. I suffer from ailments, and this facility was what I needed. If you need help, YOU NEED TO STOP IN COATESVILLE. These PROFESSIONALS will help! Thank you all.

I don't usually post reviews but this place needs to be held accountable for its terrible and overall unqualified services. The problems with this facility go beyond the time spent there. I went to therapy (out patient) once a week for about 5 months and it did not help. I originally shrugged it off assuming therapy just may not be a good treatment plan for me. A year later I am ready to see a different therapist at a different organization which has led to an annoying 2 month process of trying to get medical records forwarded along. After a month of not hearing anything I had to reach out to the office to see what the issue was, they explained the paperwork sent over from original visit at another facility was not valid and I would need to fill out theirs. 6 phone calls and two days later someone finally emails me a copy of the forms. Then I had to continuously call to check on the progress between two departments because apparently the medical release office and the out patient office don't communicate effectively with an estimate of 30 business days to have my records released. When I was finally able to speak to outpatient instead of a voice mail I was told they never received the request that was supposedly sent over a week ago. The woman out patient was able to have my stuff processed and it is said to be in transit to the other facility which would take a week or so because they don't fax things just postal sending (IN 2018?????). As soon as I think this situation is resolved I receive a $200 bill for services from a year ago out of no where. Spoke to my insurance and they explained they will be reviewing the bill again and they the facility should not be billing me directly for anything especially this far past the date of treatment. They may be taking advantage of people with these delayed bills especially since the majority of their patients are seeking help for mental illnessTLDR: The service is abysmal at best during outpatient treatment. the headache doesn't end after leaving this place especially if you need medical records released. The staff does not communicate or seem to care about doing their job whatsoever. If you receive a delayed bill contact your insurance to clear things up, Belmont or their collection agency will not be helpful.

Unfortunately, my 14 year old daughter is there now.  This place is horrible.  Please do not allow your family to be transported there under any circumstance#1 - At admission, they separated us and had her sign documents with out me present.  This is allowed in Pa since consent is at the age of 14.  The rules were not fully understood by my daughter who believed that she could say when she wants to leave#2 - They did not have any history about her nor did they want me to provide it.   I believe that this is rather critical in providing mental health care.  Especially when you have had support from exceptional doctors at CHOP for the past 3 years#3 - When we were finally able to talk to our daughter after about about 48 hours, we learned that they never provided her with her medication!  This is absolutely astounding and borderline malpractice.  Worse, we verbally told them the requirements at admission and we talked to the social worker at 24 hours#4 - My daughter is terrified of needles.  She requires a sedative and plenty of emotional support to get her through the process.  Still, this is an ordeal for her.  A social worker and a physician at Belmont were told this.  It is in her records.  No of that mattered.  They drew blood with out her consent and no sedative provided#5 - It took a day for them to figure out that we dropped off clothes for her.  Those were only provided after she explicitly asked for them#6 - After 36 hours, they have decided to change her diagnosis from one that was previously established over 3 years by extremely qualified doctors at Children’s Hospital.  On top of that, they want to completely change her medications.  See points #2 and #3 above.  Again, this is extremely alarmingMany of you will read bad reviews about clinics.  You may come to the conclusion that those who write them are former patients.  The reviews may be foggy and come across as disgruntled.  In this case, I am a very alarmed parent of a girl who needs help.  I do not believe that this facility is helping herAgain, do not go there.

My Dad is a 97 year old Vet and the Coatesville VA treats him like gold in every instance to -date. I was wondering, seeing long lines in other states of seniors on the news waiting to get the vaccine, how in the world I would get my dad a vaccine since he is not in a nursing home. I care for him at home. He can't wait in lines like that. He is on 02 and incapable physically to do that. Well, yesterday he got a call from the VA with an appt. for his first Moderma vaccine. WOW! I drove him there today and I have never seen a more efficient set up. No lines. No wait. Everyone so efficient and kind. They didn't even have him use his walker to get to the room where the covid vaccines were being given. They gave us a wheel chair and even had a volunteer push him instead of me doing it. I had full confidence in the safety of the set up in regard to covid. Wonderful! And the protocol for giving the shots - fantastic. Every single thing was well planned out and carried out to perfection. Thank you VA for this. I am so so pleased. He has his appt for his next shot already. FABULOUS. Worth 100 stars.

I had only great experiences with doctors and staff.  Everyone I encountered so far was professional,  pleasant and extremely helpful.   It took me a while to get used to it, since I more often than not have to stand up for the services I should be able to expect.

All I can say is and with confidence, this facility by far tops a majority of these state run places. I mean detox alone had hotel style beds, real comforters and pillows. Opiates was my demise and I'll tell you what, for over 15 years of addiction of opiates, this was the first and only place where my detox actually when really smooth. Overall just a clean and neat place. People are going to always have certain negative things to say... unfortunately that's today's society. No facility is by any means perfect. Yeah livengrin for sure needs to hire some employees but for what it's worth....they do the best they can and I've always appreciated the things they do....and lastly shout out to ABE!!! Hands down best counselor and growing up how I did...coming to find out him and I sort of walked down similar path as kids so he helped me more than words could ever explain. THANKS!

I was admitted to Livengrin in April 2017. From the first step in the door I was greatly cared for. The detox nurses have an EXTREMELY tough job and I felt they always gave me the best care and treated me with respect. The clinical director tried so hard to work with my probation officer to not violate me and send me upstate. Needless to say, I was extremely upset over this news and the clinical staff worked with me and help me cope with this news.  By the time I left I was accepting of my future. I learned alot. So many times in recovery we lose ourselves so much, we don't know how to get it back.  Livengrin helped me regain structure. Getting up, making your bed, keeping a clean room all plays into recovery. They were always respectful to my family and my now husband at family visits. They helped them understand addiction and what I needed.  I owe my life to Livengrin. Everyday after lunch, I'd go out into the Serenity Garden and meditate. The program there is amazing. Thanks to Livengrin I now have 26 months sober, working at a rehab, custody of my kids back and happily married. Recovery does work if you WANT IT!!!!  Thank you LIVENGRIN for giving my family back their daughter, wife and mother! I'd love to come back and share my story!!!

I went to the hospital because I was very far off from being well and by the time I was brought to the room I would be staying in I was much worse off physically, mentally and emotionally than when I showed upThe treatment team that I had was less than willing to work with me towards any positive treatment goals and I was told by the unit psychiatrist that they would not be able to help me there but would not release me until I signed a 72 order and completed those 72 hours in the unit. I was without most of my medications to manage multiple physical issues. Getting my psych. medications was also an issue for the first several days, my medication for anxiety and PTSD was never addressed causing me to be in a complete state of panic for the time that I was inpatient (7 days). The medical staff was non-existent in helping me with addressing my medical problems (had surgery right before going inpatient). I was physically ill the majority of time that I was there and getting Imodium to address those issues took over 24 hours for approval and getting them added to my chartGroup therapy was limited to movement and musical therapy. Two things I could not do, because of the recent surgery and the migraine I was experiencing and unable to get medication forThe whole time I was there I was in more pain, physically, mentally and emotionally. Everything about what they do in regards to caring for people with mood and mental disorders needs to be reevaluated as they are doing more harm than good to a very vulnerable population.

I won't go into details. The doctor was condescending and skeptical of my condition. I felt so uncomfortable and unwelcomed that I ended up walking out. The nurse jokingly reminded me to come back to the ER in the future whenever I like. I immediately went to a civilian hospital where I was found to have an infection from a prostate biopsy that I received from the VAMC. Long story short, ultimately I had to stay in the hospital for a few days due to a bad infection, received some antibiotics intravenously but I  was unable to sleep for 3 days due to the persistent migraines which accompanied  my iinfection. To be fair to the VAMC Emergency Room staff, it does seem that too many hospitals have obtained a reputation for ignoring the spirit of their Professional Oath. It appears that for a profession that is the highest paid of all professions, generally speaking, many have become far too self-important and cynical in a profession that requires compassion, understanding and priority to the patient.

Our loved one Vanessa Walls transitioned suddenly on Wednesday October 18. 2017, she was a Faithful, Active, Serving and Tithe paying member of  Victory Christian Center Church under the Leadership of Apostle Jimmie A. Ellis. We as her family planned to have her Celebration of Life at the church since this was her home church that she loved dearly.  To our surprise this Organization was HORRIBLE!! No one came to pray with or comfort her family. No one came to visit her 80year old mother whose daughter was faithful to their ministry. Per their website they “While providing spiritual guidance, VCC also offers a broad range of assistance and support services to meet the physical, financial, educational and emotional needs of its members and community through more than 40 church ministries and programs” WHERE?? We received a phone call on Monday October 23, 2017 from the Church Administrator Betty Liggon who stated that the date we needed was not confirmed with the church to have a Homegoing Service. Okay, fine. We then went to the church for a meeting. During this meeting we were offered no comfort, no hospitality, no prayers, tissue or even water!!This meeting seemed more of an inconvenience for the church then a help to serve the family of one of their faithful members who passed away. Did I say no one came to the house or offered prayers or condolences?? Okay back to this meeting. In the meeting we were told by Betty Liggon what the fee would be to hold the service and that she didn’t know if the church would be available, nor if they had anyone to accommodate us on that day. When the fact that no one came to the home of the deceased, Mrs Liggon stated “Oh we called, we don’t just go to people’s houses!”…Excuse me?? Okay lets move on the fact that they didn’t know who could be availible because as we were told 95% of their members WORK. Well where is the other 5%? We were then asked “Well what does the family need we will send someone out today on Monday October 23, 2017 5days after my Aunt passed. The church leadership knew of her passing on October 18 as Dr. Virginia Booth created a Facebook post. So why hasn’t this church reached out or even tried to offer any Christlike Assistance to this family!! Upon the closing of the meeting I asked If Thursday October 26, 2017 was now a confirmed date with VCC, Mrs Liggon assured us YES this is now a confirmed dateWe left the church and proceeded to go to the Funeral Home to finalize the arrangements. About 15 minutes after leaving the church we received a call from Betty Liggon (Deaconess/Church Administrator) who then told us that VCC would not be able to accommodate us to lay their member and our loved one to rest. When asked for an explanation she stated that “since the family is already displeased with the church they can’t accommodate us”. Dr. Wesley Proctor then snatched the phone and very flamboyantly stated “ WE JUST NOT GONNA TOUCH IT”….It??? IT??? IT??? You mean the home going celebration of a faithful member who pressed her WAY to Worship and Serve at this Church!!!....A faithful member who got off her sick bed against Doctors orders to come to church and worship… A faithful tithe paying member who has passed out on the street going to and from this Church!! We were TREATED HORRIBLY AS A GRIEVING FAMILY!!THERE WAS NO CHRISTIAN COMPASSION SHOWN TOWARD MY FAMILY AT ALL!!! So all of these things this Ministry states that they are is INDEED FRAUDUELNT!!! SO this is the treatment you get as a member of this “Place of Worship” when you die. THEY ARE MONEY HUNGRY AND A DISGRACE TO THE BODY OF CHRIST!!!There is more but I just dont have the energy right now!! A DISGRACE VICTORY CHRISTIAN CENTER OF PHILADELPHIA CHURCH IS!!!...If you are offened PLEASE BE VERY OFFENDED...AS A GRIEVING FAMILY WE ARE BEYOND OFFENDED...Instead of creating a resolve they as a CHURCH chose \"NOT TO TOUCH IT\" as Dr. Wesley Proctor stated!!PLEASE SHARE!!!

I wanted to take my time and look at all the reviews before writing my own. I am surprised at some of the comments I've seen. I was admitted to Livengrin on 3/26 and completed my stay on 4/25. I can honestly say that Livengrin gave me no reasons to complain. They saved me lifeI've visited other sites before but my experience at Livengrin was amazing. Everyone from admissions, staff techs, patient care coordinators, nursing, counselors and even staff from the kitchen were all wonderful, caring, and kind. I came into this facility emotionally broken and if it weren't for the big hearts that each of them displayed while I was there, I don't know where I would be at right now, let alone, competent enough to even write this reviewTruth be told, the people in the rehab community I saw that might have a complaint would be the ones that did not really want to be treated. I was able to get a general view of who truly wanted to recover and those that just were going through the motions. I pray for all of them. Recovery is something that you would have to do on your own and Livengrin provides the resources and the tools to GUIDE you back to that road. I am happy to say that I'm on that path thanks to them again and it's going to be up to me to stay on it. I am forever grateful to have been admitted to this facility and to have crossed paths with everyone in there. I look forward to now joining their Alumni Association and coming back one day to inspire hope for the next group that wants to recover.

I can't stress enough how much you should avoid placing your loved ones in the care of this horrific excuse for a hospital. My son (12) was placed here when he was in a bad way because there were no beds available at any other facility. It was his first hospitalization and he was terrified and hysterical. The staff were utterly devoid of compassion. They called him names. They fat-shamed him. When I questioned all of this after my son's first night, I was told the staff had a \"tough love\" approach. But that is nothing more than a blatant attempt to excuse the behavior of an unfit staff. They cursed at the children. They did nothing to stop other patients from verbal bullying or physical aggression. The staff did not get physical with my son directly but he reported they did with other children, even knocking down a disabled boy.  They also got my son's meds wrong, or rather repeatedly omitted one, despite my insistence over several days. They didn't bother giving him the personal items we brought (even though they were allowed items). I confronted one of the nurses who had shamed him for crying and she gave me some B.S. excuse for her behavior and then gave me the silent treatment in order to end the conversation. It was beyond unacceptable. I could really go on and on. My troubled son left here after a week in far worse shape than he came in. He twitched like a PTSD survivor for days afterward. I believe the psychiatry staff to be well-meaning, but the staff on the unit floor had no business working with children, and especially not mentally unstable children. My son has since been hospitalized elsewhere, and several other children related that Belmont was the worst of every facility they'd been in. Aren't there accrediting organizations governing such facilities? This one ought to have its accreditation revoked.

RHD Fernwood  is a place which give homeless women and men a place to live while they get their affairs back in order.Thier are many different reasons for needing this service.The parking lot and building are wheelchair accessible.There is a security check point upon enteringResidents ,both men and women are offered many different servicesduring their stay . Housing is the main goal . Many residents are starting over after losing everything.Casemangers and Staff offer guidence and support to people who are in need of itJob training and employment information are posted when available. Housing information is also posted when availableWhile staying at Fernwood  residents are given a safe place to stay. Three meals a day are provided. Clothing can be obtained from donations if needed.There are separate floors for men and women.Both floors have shower rooms. There is a large room where meals and meetings are held . There is also a smaller room with a T.V. to watch movies or read a book.Residents are given rooms with privacy and a bathroom for their personal useFernwood is a non- smoking facility. Residents have access to medical assistance if needed.Help with transportation to appointments when possible . Public transportation is close byIn closing Fernwood is one of the best shelters you can be placed at .

If you really need & want help, be grateful for having somewhere safe to go. No, it's not a 5 Star Hotel. But it's not Hell either. Nobody & nowhere is perfect. The admission process took 4ever, but I was given something decent to eat & drink. I was placed in the totally wrong unit. The D&A UNIT. I didn't, thankfully, have D&A Issues, but no beds were available anywhere else. Told I'd be moved when a bed was available. Never was. I dealt with it. I was safe. The paint was peeling, the chairs were torn up. I  still had somewhere safe to sleep, eat, shower. It was clean in my room. The woman that cleaned it daily was wonderful & very pleasant & kept our room (3 to a room) clean & well stocked w/all necessities. (Altho, I still wonder WHY U can't see out of the windows in the patients rooms??).  I met some of the most wonderful, amazing, beautiful PPL here, Learned alot about D&A issues & struggles, & could relate to almost all of them. D&A & MH issues & struggles are very similar. We all have fears, problems, (whether it be financial, family, social, legal, relationship, etc)  & deal w/\"LABELS, stigma, attitudes, Society.The staff tries their best. My Dr. was great. Straightened out my meds quickly. The staff treated me how I treated them. W/respect & kindness. Yes, they weren't always the nicest. They have alot of patients & responsibilities. 1 staff member I'd like to acknowledge is *KIMBERLY*! THANK U! U R WONDERFUL!! I was weary about being in the D&A Unit,. But got to know lots of ppl & staff. & Am so grateful to have met them. They have a wonderful Music Therapy Program, daily. Thanks to the 2 wonderful therapists that ran this group. Their playing various musical intruments and Beautiful singing was powerful and Soo beneficial to me & others. We got to really participate & share our feelings, strengths, weaknesses, hopes, etc. & Thanks to the AA & NA members who ran the meetings everyday. Thanks for sharing your stories. Even tho I wasn't on the unit I should have been on, I learned alot, was safe, gained so much wisdom about many differents issues I face daily. It's still a struggle... But it's what you make of it. Take & learn what you can from this place. I got good & bad from different staff members. They give you a ride home @discharge. If U don't have a \"home\", they try their 'best' to find you one, even if it's a homeless shelter. I realize (& I  know 1st hand), that ALOT of mental health \"Professionals\" (& other \"Professionals\" or \"Non-Professionals\" in any field) are far from \"Professional\". Report them ASAP, Have them arrested, sue them, make them lose their jobs! We all deserve to be treated fairly & humanely by everyone & everywhere we go or end up. & Make sure you give them all Ur insurance info. I'm still getting bills from them to this day. & They have all my info. So, no, I'm not paying anything that my insurance does or should.

I did watch, however, and I liked how the kids can do this much stuff in a game like 2K or Madden. Either way, I mainly just drawed a lot, or played board games like Connect 4 or SorryAnyways, I liked being here and being away from my problems I had at home, and note, this was my 2nd Hospital Stay, so I already expected a lot from being here. I had 4 in a matter of 3 weeks and nobody claimed to know about the previous ones.! I really appreciate the fine staff and services provided by VA Medical Center, Coatesville, PA. Ask a question in the hallway and you may get several staffers to stop and assist. The Ophthalmology Department, Geriatric Department, and Coordinated Care program have combined to insure the Veteran's Administration Healthcare System has got my back. That means so much. Recently however with the new COVID-19 cautions, my experience with the routing of traffic into the Medical Center suggests a possible need for improvement. Upon turning into the usual entry, I was met with a ridiculously loud police horn. Big police presence.  I could read the sign \"EXIT\" and was obviously turning around. Yet someone behind the dark windows laid on the big horn un-necessarily. I found the entry point and proceeded through the well planned route for testing and screening for COVID-19. With clear instructions, I continued the course to an assigned  parking area quite a long hike away from building #1, my appointment location. Many veterans have mobility issues. I don't know how they are expected to get to an appointment without a shuttle of some sort.  Schlepping back from my appointment, I ran across a fellow Vietnam Veteran, a Marine. I ask him did he know why we were parked so far away.  He laughed and we  shrugged with snafu recognition. He then said, \"Some cop  in a huge police van really gave me the big horn when I pulled in to turn around.\" We both laughed and did the brig step back to our cars.  I am Michael Malsbary USMC 1963-1967 3/7/3  Chu Lai RVN 1965- Operation Starlight- August 1965- Op Black Ferret, Op Piranha, Op Harvest Moon.  First MARS op in RVN N0EFD.  Semper FI

Ok, so maybe i didn't love it from day one.  I was not used to people telling me where i needed to go, and when.  And i certainly wasn't used to LISTENING to them!  But after a while, i realized that doing what i wanted was getting me nowhere but into a downward spiral.  And when I finally stopped fighting, and started following directions, i realized that the staff there ALL wanted me to get well, and really cared about me.  In my experience, the patients who thought that this was their private country club and wanted to run their own show, were the ones who got pissed off and bolted treatment.  Frankly, that just helped those of us who really wanted to be there and get well.  It's been four months since i got out of Livengrin, and 2 months since i finished my outpatient treatment.  Yeah, going to meetings 3 or 4 times a week is a drag sometimes, but it is what keeps me clean today--almost 5 months without a drug or a drink and happier than i have been since i can remember!

This program was very rewarding, including many important methods, handouts, exercises, and interactions between both peers and therapist professionals. Both group therapy and individualized therapy sessions were very beneficial and an excellent opportunity to rehabilitate and reintegrate ourselves into the community. Therapists and social workers also went out of their way even in a short period of time to assist in locating volunteer opportunities, and motivating patients to comply with their treatment plans. I feel that my discharge process is and will finish smoothly, that I am encouraged to remain productive, feel safe, and much better than when I first began the program as a new patient. It feels very good to have mental health advocates that support, mentor, and provide critical care and follow-up care, correspondences, and are motivated to serve as a liaison for patient's success! Productive leadership goes a long way, and this is demonstrated in the ways the program is run. Belmont has really set the bar high in this wayThank you for all that you do, and employing, training, and maintaining staff that always go above and beyond for patients.

I cut short this very important hospitalization due to the facilities cleanliness, rodent issue, and NUMERIOUS concerns of mistreatment by the BHA staff to my child and other children. Once this test was administered by staff she then had a bad mental reaction and the BHA staff then manhandled my child to subdued her to sedate her, this also happened without any paperwork submitted to her file which was admitted to me in the exit meeting as well and was to be looked into by the nursing supervisor. They also continued to given my vegetarian child meat throughout the stay as well as the basic cleanliness is just not up to standards on a child's unit. My child was there for DEPRESSION not behavioral issues and has never exhibited any behavioral issues, this place is unsuitable which is terrible because the Dr.'s and therapists actually care, they just cant contain the minute to minute staff.

Erik Robertson is by far the best physical therapist I’ve ever worked with. I am an avid climber- and after needing wrist surgeries 8 years ago- I fell into a slew of physical issues that persisted and compiled for years. I have gone to my share of PTs. Literally no one can hold a candle to what Erik does. He’s knowledgeable and makes sure you are educated on your treatment plan. His diagnosis protocol is impressive- and the exercises he gives help! He does a lot of pain management during his sessions as well that help further success- such as cupping, stretches for mobility, massage etcHe also is very good at providing a comfortable and accepting environment. I always leave my appointments feeling heard and seenWithin a few months of working with him I am finally getting my life back.

This place is good if your looking for shelter and basic mental health services for very minor mental health issues. For a program that only supports youth aged 18-21 they provide very little help with issues beyond work, housing, and identification services. Their mental health treatment is as bare as possible dealing with simple issues like anxiety or depression but little for bigger issues like schizophrenia or bipolar and give little help pushing you towards a program to help support you in that aspect. As a simple homeless shelter it is pretty good with  a reasonable food schedule even though if strictly enforced. I would give this a higher score if it was simply a normal homeless shelter but as it is aimed towards younger adults and getting them off the streets they offer little support aside from finding you housing and getting you jobs, in fact they will sometimes enforce times where you cannot enter the building without a job which in my opinion defeats the purpose of getting youth off the street if your restricting them from a safe environment. For a place that helps abandoned youth transition into adulthood very little support is givenSummary: Overall it's a good homeless shelter but a subpar young adult support shelterPros:-Good rooming situations and provided washing and dryer-Access to a reliable meal schedule-Chores to teach responsibility-Provide helpful job opportunities-Good clinical help with minor mental health issuesCons:-Little assistance to major health issues (Schizophrenia, Bipolar, etc.)-Strict rules on scheduled times-Slow reception on housing opportunities together with pressured limited time shelter stay-Minor help on services beyond identification and jobs-Little encouragement of youth provided
