I was hired back during the beginning of February of this year for the inpatient social work position, and then terminated a month in a half later. My “fellow” colleague who I was training under set me up to be terminated from this position. She reduced my caseload to seem like it was too much for me to handle. During my time there this colleague was rude to me because according to her I asked too many questions. For what I thought was a routine check to see how everything is coming along it turn out to be my termination. The director of the program never gave me a warning. Just stated that I was being terminated because she thought that I couldn’t handle the caseload due to former my colleague purposely sabotaging it. I thought that the director of the program could have given me a warning or ask me more questions in regards to how everything is coming along. I looked forward my whole career working a position like this. And for it to be literally snatched from beneath me was disheartening. For any new incoming employees especially hired for a social worker please keep a list of  the clients that you work with and make copies of paper work to be on the safe side. That’s what I should have done.

PLEASE DO NOT SEND YOUR LOVED ONES HERE, ESPECIALLY ADOLESCENTS. THEY WILL LEAVE WORSE THAN WHEN THEY WENT IN. These reviews are no joke. This hospital is horrid. I stayed here in 2010, when I was 13, for four days. There is no true help provided here. My goal became not to get better, but to get OUT of this place. You only got to see an actual psychiatrist for maybe 5 minutes at most. They put you on all different kinds of medicines without telling you why. I had a male nurse unlock and come into my room at night when I was sleeping and inject medicine into my arm without even fully waking me up. You were not allowed to shower without someone keeping their foot in the door and checking on you. I was there for a suicide attempt but there were other children in with me for many different things like substance abuse. There was no direction or kindness from staff. I am currently an adult in therapy working on trauma from this facility. Please deeply reconsider using this hospital.

First, I want to start by saying the provider I had at Mazzoni was fantastic. Would totally recommend himBut why the one star? Because the office is managed horribly, and they have no idea what is going on. I called for a sick appointment, was told I'd hear back in 24 hours, heard back 2 weeks laterI had a follow up appointment and received no less than 10 text messages that it was cancelled. Even called to find out if it was and they said no, and I continued to receive messages it was cancelled. So I didn't go and received a nasty-gram from them about missing appointmentsNever can get anyone on the phone in a reasonable amount of time. I've called and been told hold times were in excess of 60 minutes. Yes, they have a callback feature, but when you wait over an hour for the callback, life happensDefinitely needs to find someone to pull this operation together. If they had strong leadership, I'd say 100% go, but you will spend more time navigating appointment setting, phone calls, messages, and attempts to see your provider than you will ever spend with your provider.

I was in the new adolescent wing almost exactly a year ago and it was probably the most traumatic part of my recovery. The \"group therapy\" sessions were beyond unorganized. Fights would break out daily and weren't broken up quickly at all. There was little food that could be considered edible in the cafeteria. One of the nurses overdosed me which left me with a dangerously low blood pressure for the entire day. This made me unable to leave my bed without passing out. They refused to bring up food for me the entire day and refused to send me back to the ER. We were treated like cattle, it was a rare occasion that you could even look out the window. Not to mention at the time, I identified as a trans boy. Instead of being supportive I was frequently demeaned and questioned about my identity. On my sheet it said I was sent for a \"med fix\", but the entire time I was there, there wasn't a single medication that was modified. When I expressed my concerns to my mother and the staff, they threatened to AMA me if I did not stay. It did more damage to me than anything and I wouldn't even send my worst enemy there. Nobody deserves to be treated like this. IF YOU CARE ABOUT YOUR LOVED ONE, DON'T SEND THEM THERE IF YOU EVER WANT THEM TO GET BETTER.

The shelter does not help with any personal needs they give you one toilet paper to use and from then on out you have to support your own. They do not have washer or drier there you have to walk to a laundry mate with your child and clothes, I wasn’t there long enough to wash clothes. They do not provide any snacks just 3 meals for the day and your not aloud to bring in outside food at a certain time. Most people who go in in the beginning have a curfew of 9pm. Very strict and not enough support. This agency does not tell you what to expect only ask if your sure you can’t go to a family member home. Surviving Mazzoni Center:Hi my name is Kymberlee and I am a single black mother whose life has been violated by Mazzoni Center. I joined therapy to have someone to express my woes, thoughts  feelings of being a single black mother. Last Thursday was my third or fourth session even meeting with my therapist and I shared with her that sometimes I have trouble taking care of myself with my depression, further explained that I make sure I take care of my daughter but whenever I am baby free I am depressed and not taking care of myself and then it’s pretty much me wearing the same clothes everyday and that I have food for my daughter to eat but due to my diet restrictions as a pescatarian, I don’t have anything for me to eat. (Sorry to shared my personal business like that but it’s the truth we’ve all been there.) My therapist offered to share some resources to help me with my situation such as Mazzoni Centers Food Bank, and then next thing I know knock I get DHS sent to my house. Dhs explained that they went to two addresses as an attempt before they got to mine and that I will now be assigned a caseworker. My therapist lied to me when I confronted her saying that I was supposed to get a phone call when the form I am reading stated an unscheduled visit. Why? Because a single black mother vented to her and now my words were taken and misconstrued into lack of supervision of my daughter. I don’t even know where they even got lack of supervision from. I am outraged and deserve justice for my daughter and I because now there is an open investigation on my peaceful household and due to the miscommunication from DHS I kept my daughter home from daycare thinking they would come visit her so that we could resolve this FALSE REPORT AND SLANDER WHICH AFFECTED  to work from home that day due to this inconvenience. I was lied to and my trust was broken. My advice is to get a journal and never trust a therapist from Mazzoni Center because they will come and try to snatch your baby if you’re going through a hard time. I wanted to share this story for any other BLACK SINGLE MOTHERS WHO HAVE BEEN IN MY POSITION AND I WANT TO PREVENT THIS EVER HAPPENING TO ANOTHER FAMILY AGAIN. My daughters father is outraged, my family is confused, and most importantly it FEELS like I will NEVER HEAL because Mazzoni Center made me stop trusting therapy, I will be contacting everyone I can to spread truth. If you are a single black woman and part of the LGBTQ+ community DO NOT TRUST THESE PEOPLE THEY JUST WANT TO BREAK FAMILIES APART AND LOCK YOU UP FOR VENTING. What I said WAS NOT A RED FLAG OR WARNING SIGN TO GET OUTSIDE FORCES INVOLVED AND I FEEL AS THOUGH A HIPPA LAW WAS BROKEN. Please protect yourself and your children from this nightmare they call a help center. They help break apart families and break your trust!! I just pray that my family stays together and strong through this difficult time.

I wish I could review their treatment, but I haven't even been able to get an appointment. I've called the outpatient referral number over a dozen times in the past two months. Turns out they don't actually have a receptionist for that line, just an answering machine. In theory they're supposed to call you back within 24 hours to schedule an intake appointment. In practice, I've gotten three or four voicemails 3-5 days after my messages (on days I specifically said I wouldn't be available during business hours due to my own work schedule), just telling me to call the same number again. The rest of my calls went completely unanswered. Eventually I tried calling the number for \"urgent matters,\" and the receptionist there said they can't schedule outpatient appointments and forwarded me back to the same answering machine yet again. I've even left messages asking them to just schedule me for any time on one of my days off in the next month and if they call while I'm at work to leave a message telling me the date and time of the appointment. I just got the same canned message yet again telling me to call the number that doesn't connect to a real personSo I can't actually review their healthcare, but as somebody who works in a medical field myself, I can tell you that Belmont has 0-star scheduling service.

I saw the reviews here and took a chance at staying here, and I entirely understand the wide spectrum in terms of reviewsI'll start by repeating what I've said to numerous people, including employees, family, and friends: you will only be treated with respect if you are coherent, calm, and complacent. All qualities not generally found in a place meant for emergency treatment for mental illnessI suppose I was lucky, with my situation coinciding with those three qualities. I won't discredit anyone's experience here, positive or otherwise, but I personally would not recommend Friends Hospital if you need genuinely anything more than a place to stay away from your current situation and are capable enough to keep yourself safe to a certain degreeWhile I was there, I experienced both ends of the human compassion spectrum, from a technician helping me contact a family member daily, to being flatly ignored when asking for items (such as my daily hygiene bag in the evenings) multiple times. I also experienced medications being given to me that were not prescribed by the psychiatrist, and witnessed emotional and verbal (sometimes physical) abuse between patients and staff alike. I am aware that mental health services as a career is draining and taxing, but I also believe that the career choice should not be taken lightly, especially by those who actively complain that other places offer more money for better hoursIn the hallway, there's a poster that details what a usual day looks like in terms of activities, all on a weekly cycle. Oftentimes, this schedule was not followed. An hour of outdoors time was not offered every day as it should have been, much less actually provided. If we did have groups, they were not done as often as it was supposed to, and the technician-led groups just did not happen. There were two choice instances where the technician-led group time slots were used, but both times were led by patients insteadThe only truly kind and friendly people I encountered were those who did not deal with patients on a constant basis, such as nurses, medical students, social workers, and admin staff. If it weren't for them, I might've done something drastic when I got homeIn all, this was absolutely not the best facility I've stayed at, and I would not recommend it to anyone who isn't excellent at pretending they are fine when at the absolute end of their rope. If you are angry, upset, confused, or anything besides calm and mildly sad, you will be met with disrespect and borderline contempt by the general staff. I truly hope that the hospital takes any and all complaints seriously, as the potential for a healing environment is there, it's just hidden under deep layers of apathy and dismissal.

it seems like this is very typical for methadone clinics, but honestly, after two years of dealing with them I deeply hate these people for consistently treating people I love like total garbage. do better. if you don’t respect the people you’re serving — then you need to get out of healthcare! methadone as a medication is the gold standard for “opioid use disorder” treatment but I don’t know if “go to a place where you’re going to be abused and treated very badly every day of your life” is good medical advice for anyone. the laws aren’t great, but employees very much use their discretion to make things much much worse than they have to be, and I truly don’t understand why. in no other area of healthcare is your medication determined by how much random employees personally like you, or based on random hoops you need to jump through every day, or your ability to skip work to wait around for hours at random with no prior notification. it’s straight up malpractice, all the time. there are a handful of good people there but they don’t have much power at all in the kafkaesque web of abuse and absurdity. go ahead and try it, almost all methadone clinics are bad and some people can make it work anyway, it can be lifesaving despite it all. I want employees to see this review moreso than potential clients (who probably already know the state of these places anyway.) but it’s such a shame how bad they are and I want to say to everyone who works here: you treated people I love like garbage and it is absolutely not okay. do better.

Very rude place and they need to be shut down. We sent my mom here so that she can feel better and she came out feeling worse! I don’t understand the ignorance and the treating people bad especially that’s reaching out for help. Shame on them!

The square itself isn't much to write home about, but it is one of the quiter areas in maybe the craziest part of the cityI the thing about McPherson Square is of course the old library that sits in the middle.
Fire alarm goes off at midnight twice a month REALLY... And SJH needs to make a lot of changes like removing a female from the kitchen who only knows how to cook hard boiled eggs.

These people deserve no, they have a bunch of underpaid people with no respect for patients.  Dr. Christian Kohler is the director of this unit and looks at everybody as being a pre-medicated pyscho.  After spending a night at hall Mercer it left me with more trauma than what the original problem was.  I spent a week on 4 spruce at Pennyslvania hospital and the people running that were very unprofessional.  I was threatened multiple times to be restrained and there was no reason for it.  I was given meds that doctor knew I had a bad reactions with previously but refused to listed due to Ego issues.  The unit smelled liked feces and urine and they regulary had people in restraints locked in a solitary room screaming for everyon,. nobody checked on these people on restraints.  4 Spruce at Pennsylvania hospital reminded me of an asylum for the mentally ill and it was a sad place,  University of Penn backs this place and someone needs to investigate everybody involved, including 'Dr. Kohler
Best place on earth.

n4203.label: Behavioral Health Services program provides mental healthcare to adults and teens diagnosed with a mental health condition, including depression, anxiety, and bipolar disorder.Services include:- Mental Health Services

n4204.label: Jefferson Health provides a free service that gives individuals with amputations the opportunity to talk with one another. The Peers share their experiences, knowledge, provide emotional support and practical information on everyday concerns. They are living proof that individuals with amputations can recover and continue to have active lives.This program provides:- Peer support- One-on-one supportThe amputee support groups takes place at 12:15 p.m. on the second Tuesday of every month.

n4205.label: Crossroads Treatment Center of Philadelphia (West Tabor) offers buprenorphine, sublocade, and brixadi treatment for individuals with Opioid Use DisorderThis program provides:- Addiction & Recovery- Medications for AddictionThis program may be full cost and/or accept some insurances, including Medicare and Medicaid. Please contact Crossroads for more information about cost

n4206.label: Crossroads Treatment Center of Philadelphia (N. Broad Street) offers Buprenorphine, Sublocade, Vivitrol, and Brixadi treatment for individuals with Opioid Use DisorderThis program provides:- Addiction & Recovery- Medications for AddictionThis program may be full cost and/or accept some insurances, including Medicare and Medicaid. Please contact Crossroads for more information about cost

n4207.label: The Center provides counseling services which help people learn how to deal more effectively with situations in their lives such as depression, addiction and substance abuse, stress, problems with self-esteem, grief, issues related to mental and emotional health, and relational problems.Services provided include:- Individual therapy - Family therapy - Group therapy - Couples therapy - Child therapy - Nutritional counselingThe Center, LLC accepts Medicare and private insurances. We are offering telehealth appointments at this time.

n4208.label: Anti-Violence Partnership (AVP) offers free, professional counseling to adult and child co-victims of homicide and those who have been traumatized by other forms of violence. This includes, but is not limited to, clients who have experienced domestic violence, aggravated assault, or robbery. These services are provided at AVP’s offices in the Art Museum Area and in West PhiladelphiaServices provided:- CounselingPlease call us to schedule an appointment as we do not accept walk-ins.

n4209.label: The Adult Outpatient Services provide assessment, diagnosis, and treatment to individuals requiring mental health or dual diagnosis treatment (treatment of mental health plus substance use). The outpatient treatment team consists of trained professionals including psychiatrists, licensed therapists, psychiatric technicians, counselors, and support staff. Focus areas include mental health and substance use assessment, psychiatric assessment and evaluation for medication, medication monitoring, limited individual counseling, group counseling, bilingual counseling services, interpreter services, benefits services, and community services referral.Common diagnoses for clients at HCCS include disorders of mood, anxiety, personality, addiction and psychosis. HCCS currently offers the following services within the traditional outpatient menu of services:- Psychiatric Evaluation- Medication Managemen- Individual Counseling- Family Therapy- Group Therapy- Psychological Testing and evaluation- Co-occurring Services - Mobile Therapy - Perinatal Depression Treatment servicesHispanic Community Counseling Services accepts Medicare and Medicaid.

n4210.label: The Education Planning and Career Development helps individuals young and old work to achieve various goals including obtaining a GED, preparing for college and standardized exams, obtaining an Associate’s or Bachelor’s Degree, building a resume and searching for a job.This program provides:- Help finding school- Help finding work - GED support and preparation - Resume writing help- Family therapy- Financial educationPlease call for more information.

n4211.label: The Child & Family Outpatient Center assesses and treats behavioral and emotional difficulties in children ages 4–21 (as long as they are still attending school) and their families. We provide various treatment modalities for children with hyperactivity, academic challenges, anxiety and/or depression. We provide: - Comprehensive assessments to determine the existence and/or degree of severity of any difficulties- Comprehensive biopsychosocial evaluations- Individual and family sessions- Boys/girls peer counseling groups- Medication management - Art, play, and traditional group therapyThe Child & Family Outpatient Center accepts Medicaid and Medicare. Potential clients need to be evaluated by WES’ Assessment & Evaluation Center in order to determine which service(s) should be utilized. For more information, please call the Assessment & Evaluation Center.

n4212.label: Crossroads Treatment Center of South Philadelphia offers Buprenorphine, Sublocade, Vivitrol and Brixadi to treat Opioid Use DisorderThis program provides:- Addiction & Recovery- Medications for AddictionThis program may be full cost and/or accept some insurances, including Medicare and Medicaid. Please contact Crossroads for more information about cost.

n4213.label: Gaudenzia offers short, moderate, and long-term Substance Use Disorder (SUD) co-occurring and mental health services to adults, women with children, homeless individuals, and individuals with chronic medical conditions.Services provided:- Short, intermediate and long term substance abuse and mental health servicesGaudenzia accepts Medicare and Medicaid.

n4214.label: Jewish Family and Children’s Service of Greater Philadelphia provides counseling services for adults. The counselors take a holistic approach as they help clients sort through feelings and work through problems. They provide a full spectrum of supportive services. Their licensed clinical social workers use several established techniques and an eclectic approach to evidence-based interventions.This program provides:- Individual and group counselingFees are established on a case-by-case basis and may include a sliding scale, Medicare and/or additional assistance, you should not let your financial situation get in the way of seeking help.

n4215.label: Using clinical protocols and activities that promote the concepts of personal strengths and resiliency, these programs combine outpatient psychiatric services with community-oriented psychiatric rehabilitation servicesMembers work on skills which will help them achieve a variety of goals ranging from psychiatric symptom reduction to independent living and vocational/educational placement. Our dedicated staff uses a blend of best practices, recovery models, and a person-centered approach that builds coping and socialization proficiency. Services are provided on-site and in a community-based setting. We also offer psychiatric resources in the form of psychiatric evaluations, medication consultation, and medication managementMembers work on specific issues of aging with a strong focus on health and wellness managementCommunity inclusion at the Packer program begins to incorporate organizations that work with an elderly population such as PHILADELPHIA CORPORATION FOR THE AGING along with various neighborhood SENIOR CENTERS.

n4216.label: Continuity of Care serves individuals who have been discharged from psychiatric hospitals and their families. We assist with:- Accessing community services- Consulting with in-patient staff- Discharge planningThe Consortium accepts Medicaid and offers a sliding scale fee for low-income individuals. We don't turn anyone away.

n4217.label: In the Outpatient Behavioral Health Department at FPCN, our licensed clinicians provide psychotherapy for adults, adolescents, and children using individual, group, couple, and family modalities.  We work to empower people to reach their life goals and are skilled in treating trauma and loss, depression, anxiety, anger management, behavior problems, stress, attention deficit, and parenting challenges.  Psychiatrists are available to those active in therapy to evaluate if medication might be helpful to a person’s treatment and to monitor its effectiveness.Services: - Mental health assessment- Therapy for Children, Adolescents, and Families- Play therapy to help children cope with emotions and learn new skills- Therapists work closely with parents or caregivers to help address the child’s needs- Medication for mental illness- Services and accommodations for people who do not speak English such as in-house staff translators or a language translation telecommunication serviceNo one will be turned away for inability to pay. FPCN offers services on a sliding scale, and accepts Medicaid and Medicare. Please bring the required documents with you for your first visit.

n4218.label: JRA delivers a 15 lbs. box of food monthly via a no-contact drop-off. This program provides:- A monthly supplemental box of kosher pantry staples and fresh produce (from October to April) delivered directly to your door by one of our friendly volunteers. (Knock, Drop, and Go). - A supplemental bag of kid-friendly food items for families with children ages 18 and under. - Donated household items, toiletries, kids' apparel (through a partnership with Cradles to Crayons) diapers and adult incontinence supplies (through a partnership with The Greater Philadelphia Diaper Bank).Some exceptions may apply. For questions, information, or to request services please contact us at (610) 660-0190 or gethelp@jewishrelief.org.

n4219.label: The Diana Baldwin Mental Health Clinic provides culturally competent behavioral health services that address the psychological, social and emotional needs of every patient. Our outpatient mental health clinic staff provide an integrated, holistic approach to therapeutic intervention.Services offered:- Individual Therapy- HIV and Trauma Informed Therapy - Women & Men’s Support Groups for people living with HIV- Referral CoordinationIf you are currently experiencing any of the following: Depression, Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD), Bipolar Disorder, Anxiety, Anger Management, Family/ Relationship problems, the Diana Baldwin Mental Health Clinic may be the right place for you.All patients are served regardless of their ability to pay. We accept both Medicaid and Medicare.

n4220.label: The Behavioral Health and Crisis Center serves children with these acute needs and to provide hope and healing in a supportive environment. The Crisis Response Center is a 24/7 walk-in facility that evaluates children 5 to 17 years old with immediate mental health needs and provides short-term therapy so the youth is safe to return home and move into outpatient therapy for follow-up care.This program provides:- Crisis responseWithin the Crisis Response Center is a three-bed Crisis Stabilization Unit for youth who need additional time and treatment to be safe. CHOP provides financial assistance for medically necessary and emergency care to patients who meet the eligibility requirements. If CHOP determines that a patient is eligible, CHOP will waive 100% of the patient’s financial responsibility (after all applicable insurances and other government assistance).

n4221.label: JEVS Community Integrated Recovery Center assists individuals who have a serious mental illness by promoting wellness, socialization, independence, community integration, education and employment to help participants achieve their goals for recovery. It includes site-based activities, training and therapy, as well as community-based activities, training and connections. JEVS offers a specialized focus for individuals with co-occurring disabilities, as well as other services including prolonged exposure therapy, mobile psychiatric rehabilitation and outpatient support.This program provides:- Career exploration and employment volunteer opportunities - Assistance to develop natural supports and use of community resources- Travel training- Psycho-education groups- Evidence-based practices, including: Cognitive Enhancement Therapy (CET)*, Prolonged Exposure and Illness Management and Recovery - Psychiatric treatment, including medication management and therapy- Bereavement/grief/loss support- Support from Certified Peer Specialists- Skills training, including coping, socialization, communication and life skills- Problem-solving and decision-making skill development - JEVS fit gym and healthy eating/cooking skills trainingJEVS provides services at no cost for those who qualify for funding, a sliding fee scale for those who are not covered by one of these funding options

n4222.label: Broad Street Love (BSL) invites anyone and everyone into their space and offers a wide range of services to meet basic needs. BSM’s commitment to radical hospitality means that hundreds of vulnerable adults are welcomed through their doors each week. This program provides:- Lunch - Mail services - Case management- Re/PreEntry services- Personal care items- Clothing- Clothes mending services

n4223.label: Friends Hospital's Adult Behavioral Health Inpatient Program helps individuals understand their condition and develop healthy alternative behaviors. The hospital has several specialized adult units to meet the varied needs of its patients. This includes an intensive adult unit for individuals with severe and persistent mental illness and our recovery unit.This program provides:- Treatment for all major psychiatric disorders and co-occurring substance issues- Recovery-oriented approach that emphasizes the patient’s support systems, strengths and community connections- Individual, group and family therapy- Individualized treatment, wellness and discharge plans- Private bedrooms with unit access to an enclosed outside courtyardAlmost all major health insurances, including Medicare are accepted. It is the policy of Friends Hospital not to create a financial hardship for any patient. If necessary, a payment plan appropriate to the patient’s financial status can be established. To make the admission process go as smoothly as possible, please download and complete the authorization completion instructions and authorization to obtain or release protected health information form. To schedule a confidential assessment or learn more about the admission process, please call the admission department.

n4224.label: Addiction Medicine and Health Advocates, Inc. (AMHA) provides evidence-based substance abuse treatment, which is continuously responsive to the needs of the patients and their families. AMHA is an outpatient facility consisting of two levels of care: Outpatient and Intensive Outpatient. Methadone Maintenance is provided on both levels of care.Services provided include:• Assessment and intake• Medication-assisted treatment (methadone maintenance)• Individual counseling & psychotherapy• Group psychotherapy• Psycho education• Medical & psychiatric evaluations • Aftercare & follow up services • Referrals (educational, medical, vocational, psychiatric, higher levels of care)• Couples & family therapy• Drug screening To begin the admissions process, please contact us. You may also come in person between the hours of 9 am and 4 pm, Monday through Friday. You will be asked to complete a short screening interview and if initial eligibility criteria is met, you will be scheduled for an assessment.At the time of your assessment, you will need to bring:• Current, state-issued photo ID• Insurance cardAMHA is a private not-for-profit organization dependent upon patient fees for its continuance. Most of our patients have some form of insurance or medical assistance that pays for medication-assisted treatment services. Individuals who do not have insurance are responsible for payment or they may be eligible for county funding.

n4225.label: As a part of a family-oriented agency, the clinic provides the most thorough approach to treatment in the least restrictive setting possible. Services include:- Psychiatric and psychological evaluations- Individual psychotherapy- Group psychotherapy- Family psychotherapy- Health rehabilitative services- Medication management- Parent and child interactive therapy- Behavioral interventions such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy- Moderate levels of crisis intervention- Wrap around\" services such as mobile therapy and in-home therapeutic support services, as neededDiagnoses served:- Severe trauma- Autism- Depression- PTSD- Other emotional or behavioral diagnosesJuvenile Justice Center Of Philadephia accepts Medicaid and Third Party insurances from most private health insurance plans for behavioral services.

n4226.label: Estadt Psychological Services work with a wide range of emotional and behavioral issues providing services that span from therapy for depression and grief counseling to parenting support, couples counseling and beyond.  This program provides:- Individual counseling- Couples counseling- Family counselingTreatment specialization includes:- Therapy for depression and anxiety- Therapy for insomnia and other sleep-related problems- Therapy for psychological issues associated with medical problems- Couples counseling- Family counseling- Parenting support- Grief counseling- Work and career issues- Stress management- Addiction & recovery- Conflict resolutionThe fee for patients who elect to not use insurance is $150 for a 55 minute session. Reduced fee services are available for private pay patients on a limited basis.

n4227.label: Behavioral Health Rehabilitative Services (BHRS), a community-based approach provided in Philadelphia, Bucks, Montgomery and Delaware Counties, helps children/youth and families develop the skills, capacity, confidence, and self-sufficiency needed to handle the challenges that life offers.Services include:- Comprehensive Evaluations by a Psychologist- Functional Behavioral Assessments- Licensed Behavioral Specialist Consultation to provide behavioral planning and intervention- Mobile Therapy to provide individual or family therapy- Therapeutic Support Services to provide 1:1 Modeling and Coaching- Connection with Medication Management Services- Transition to natural supports and other appropriate services as neededBHRS are provided in the home, school and/or community for those children and youth who need more support than outpatient services can offer to maintain stability in one or more life domain while also developing the natural supports needed to sustain the skills gained.

n4228.label: Pennsylvania Mentor provides Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services (BHRS) to children with behavioral and emotional disorders and to children and youth living with Autism and Autism Spectrum Disorder. We work one-on-one with the child to help them grow and learn to the best of their abilities.Services offered:- Mentoring- Behavioral Health- Rehabilitation- Skill development- Therapeutic treatmentPennsylvania Mentor accepts Medicaid, and no one will be turned away regardless of ability to pay.

n4229.label: The Cohen's Veteran's Network provides clinical services to veterans, active duty military, and their families. This program provides high-quality, accessible and integrated mental health care.This program provides:- Evidence based therapy services This program also provides support and referrals for related needs including unemployment, housing, finance and education. They also offer in-home services, telehealth and childcare are offered on a clinic-by-clinic basis. Please check the nearest clinic for more information on provided services.

n4230.label: ATA specializes in providing mental health services to children and adolescents with severe behavior problems, histories of physical abuse, sexual abuse, or neglect, child or adolescent sexual abusers and/or substance abuse issues. This program provides: - Victim services- Forensic foster care- Family servicesATA accepts medicaid and medicare.

n4231.label: Cast Your Cares Ministry's Food Cupboard provides groceries for individuals and families in need. People know they can come to Cast Your Cares to receive food and not just a referral.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needsDue to the COVID-19 pandemic, grocery pickup is by appointment only. Please stop by and sign up to receive an appointment for groceries on Tuesdays between 2-3PM.

n4232.label: The Children’s Blended Case Management Unit provides assistance for children 6 to 17 years of age (21 years if still enrolled in school) with a behavioral or mental health diagnosis, or intellectual disability in the community or residential treatment facility setting with low, moderate, and severe needs.Services include:- 24 hour-a-day, 7 day-a-week crisis responseThe program’s goals are to connect children to services and resources which will help with their recovery and improvement, collaborate with service providers to monitor consumer participation and satisfaction, help coordinate existing services, and to advocate and provide additional support for the child. The Consortium accepts Medicaid and offers a sliding scale fee for low-income individuals. We don't turn anyone away.

n4233.label: The Family Practice and Counseling Network’s Dental Department is dedicated to providing high quality, affordable, and comprehensive oral health services to a culturally diverse community. The Dental staff is comprised of committed professionals who enjoy working together to support our patients, each other, and our mission.Preventative services provided include:- Comprehensive and periodic oral exams- Digital x-rays- Emergency treatment- Adult and children’s cleanings- Sealants- Fluoride treatment- Space maintainers- Tobacco counseling- Oral hygiene instructionRestorative and comprehensive services provided include:- Fillings - amalgam (silver) and composite (tooth colored)- Crowns (caps)- Bridges- Dentures- Extractions- Periodontal cleaning (scaling)- Pulpotomies (Emergency root therapy)- Tooth whitening (bleaching)We accept Medicaid, Medicare, and most major insurances. We also participate with many PPO plans and offers sliding fees for our uninsured patients.

n4234.label: CRISIS/MATES provide short–term home and community based intervention services to children and adults who are experiencing a behavioral health crisis. The crisis staff conducts an assessment in the community to determine the appropriate level of care. The teams are prepared to handle a variety of interventions including:- Crisis assessments- Involuntary/voluntary hospitalizations- Linkage with other mental health providers depending on medical coverage- Mobile mental health treatment services (for individuals who are temporarily homebound due to existing medical conditions or specialized mental health disorders)- Emergency psychiatric services for those who are eligibleAll services are confidential and handicap accessible transportation is available. Individuals can be referred to the program by family members, schools, public agencies and/or the Office of Mental Health.

n4235.label: CATCH's Residence is an eight bed program designed for consumers who are homeless and have psychiatric or psychiatric/substance abuse issues. Using the Mental Health Recovery Model, their highly trained staff treats mental health/substance abuse issues and facilitates the transfer of consumers to longer care transitional housing programsThis program provides:- Residential treatment for mental health/substance dependency issuesThe length of stay and treatment is 30 to 90 days.

n4236.label: Using clinical protocols and activities that promote the concepts of personal strengths and resiliency, these programs combine outpatient psychiatric services with community-oriented psychiatric rehabilitation servicesMembers work on skills which will help them achieve a variety of goals ranging from psychiatric symptom reduction to independent living and vocational/educational placement. Our dedicated staff uses a blend of best practices, recovery models, and a person-centered approach that builds coping and socialization proficiency. Services are provided on-site and in a community-based setting. We also offer psychiatric resources in the form of psychiatric evaluations, medication consultation, and medication managementMembers –build off of the development and social maturity of its participants in order to help them actively integrate into local community events and activities.

n4237.label: Our Behavioral Health Outpatient Services offers services for adults, children, and families that are dealing with mental health issues. Our experienced, compassionate caregivers help each patient find the treatment options that will work best for their unique situation.Our services include:- Evaluation and treatment- A safe, caring treatment environment- Individualized plans for each our clients- Professional and trained staff John F. Kennedy Behavioral Health Center accepts Medicaid.

n4238.label: Our Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) offers intensive and individualized services that integrate therapeutic interventions and academic support. The majority of students referred typically display behavioral challenges that manifest as multiple school suspensions, poor academic performance, impulsivity, and difficulty responding appropriately to school structure and authority figures. One of the primary objectives of Northern’s PHP programs is to decrease behaviors that interfere with classroom learning and problem solving.Our programs offer: - Intensive and individualized therapeutic interventionsParticipants of this program will also receive individual therapy, family and group therapy skills, social skill development, homework help and tutoring, and job counseling. We do accept Medicaid and Medicare for this program.

n4239.label: South Philadelphia Community Fridge is a volunteer-based organization dedicated to giving all Philadelphians access to fresh and healthy food, combatting food insecurity and inequality in our city through mutual aid. 6 community fridge and pantry locations are stocked with produce, pantry staples, and prepared foods. This program provides: - Food including produce, pantry staples and prepared foods. Please visit the website for the most up to date list of locations. Note some locations are pantries only and do not have refrigerated items. Pantry and fridge stock may vary.

n4240.label: Sayre Health Center's Behavioral Health Services' provide trauma-sensitive counseling services to all patients. Sayre Health Center's experienced, compassionate specialists help each patient find the treatment options that will work best for their unique situation.This program provides: - Counseling- Referral to psychiatry- Case management - Assistance with quitting smoking

n4241.label: Our Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Service provides holistic, child-centered, and family focused services for school-age children and adolescents with complicated and serious behavioral health needs, including Autistic Spectrum Disorder. The BHRS Department is comprised of a highly trained team of professionals who use a variety of therapeutic skills in a community setting to help consumers achieve their goals. In addition, consumers may be offered, as part of their treatment, psychiatric/ psychological evaluations, psychological testing and medication management conducted by our board certified psychologist and, if appropriate, a board certified child psychiatrist.Services Provided:- In Home Therapeutic Support Staff – Provides the bulk of the BHRS Services administered outside of school by working to redirect the child to more appropriate behaviors or responses based on what is called for in the treatment plan- In School Therapeutic Support Staff – Provides the bulk of the BHRS Services administered during school hours by working to redirect the child to more appropriate behaviors or responses based on what is called for in the treatment plan- Mobile Therapist – Provides individual therapy to the child in a non-traditional setting such as the home or some other community setting- Behavioral Specialist Consultant – Responsible for writing the treatment plan and working with the MT and TSS to implement, update and carry out the objectives of the treatment planEligibility: - Program is for patients age 5 to 13- Must be a Philadelphia residentConsumers are referred to us by CBH. We also accept self referrals.

n4242.label: Drug & Alcohol Detox program offers detox in non-hospital, medical-monitored program for individuals who don’t have a high risk of intense or life-threatening withdrawal symptoms, but still have detox needs.We provide:- Detox- Drug screening- CounselingLivengrin Foundation accepts BlueCross BlueShield.

n4243.label: The Room hosts a free food pantry to serve the members of the community fight hunger. This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs

n4244.label: Pinn Memorial Baptist Church helps alleviate hunger in the community in an environment of respect, care and hospitality.This program provides:- Foods to meet basic nutritional needsFood is distributed every Wednesday from 10am - 2pm.

n4245.label: Community Residential Rehabilitation Services program assists individuals with chronic a mental illness live in a group home setting. Services provided are evidenced-based and follow several key priorities including an individual’s willingness to participate in rehabilitation treatment, the need for structure, support, assistance to live and cooperate with others, and demonstrate the ability to live independently without intensive supports.This program provides:- Residential living- Daily life skills- Peer support- Rehabilitation treatmentHorizon House accepts Medicare and Medicaid. For services in Chester County, please call Chester County Department of Mental Health/Intellectual & Developmental Disabilities at 610-344-6265 or 800-692-1100, ext. 6265. For services in Delaware County, please call Delaware County Office of Behavioral Health at 610-713-2365. For services in Philadelphia County, please call Horizon House Residential Services at phone: 215-386-3838, ext. 12224.

n4246.label: Interact provides effective and evidence-based assessments and treatment to persons of all ages with an autism spectrum disorder. n4277.label: The CRC is a 24-hour psychiatric emergency service where individuals experiencing behavioral health crises receive evaluation, treatment and referral for additional care. We treat episodes of psychosis, intoxication, substance withdrawal, and threatened or attempted suicide, among other conditions.Patients can receive the following services: - Evaluation & stabilization- Medical treatment when necessary- Medication to calm or stabilize them, as necessary- Access to our six-bed, 23-hour observation unit for those who require more time for assessment Our goal is to refer patients to the most appropriate services after discharge, which may include: - The inpatient Behavioral Health Center onsite at Episcopal Campus- Other agencies and facilities throughout the Delaware Valley- An inpatient substance use program- Outpatient care- Homeless shelters or recovery housesThe CRC accepts walk-in patients experiencing a behavioral health crisis, or they may receive treatment at the center at the direction of medical providers or law enforcement authorities. Patients may access the CRC by visiting the Episcopal Campus emergency room. Upon arrival at the CRC, patients undergo an initial interview with a nurse or mental health technician, followed by a second, more detailed discussion with an attending psychiatrist or psychiatry resident. Temple Health's Episcopal Campus accepts Medicaid and Medicare, and offers Financial Assistance & Charity Care (free or reduced-priced care) to uninsured and/or low-income persons who qualify.

n4288.label: At Horizon House we focus on an individual’s re-socialization and engage the entire community—other residents, staff, and social context—as components of treatment. n4316.label: The Adult Outpatient program provides assessment and treatment services to individuals aged 18 and older with behavioral health concerns.
