We don't turn anyone away. If you are feeling sad, depressed, angry or anxious, we can help.  Same or next day appointments for intake are available.

n4317.label: Merakey's MCRSS program is designed to help those in crisis work through the crisis and maintain their mental health in our 8 week stabilization program. This program is open to clients ages 5-21 experiencing mental health issues. If you are in crisis, please contact 570-224-9002 for immediate response. If you are inquiring about stabilization services, please contact the number listed for the main office.

n4318.label: Greater Philadelphia Asian Social Service Center (GPASS) offers a food pantry site that distributes free food to residents in the Olney/Logan area and its neighboring communities.  Recipients must apply and meet eligibility requirements to receive food from the Food Program and Senior Food Box.  We do not discriminate against anyone because of sex, age, religion, ethnic or racial background, nationality, and/or physical appearance.  GPASS has the right to deny services without discrimination to an individual.This program provides:- Food to meet nutritional needsRecipients must apply and meet eligibility requirements to receive food from the Food Program. GPASS does not discriminate against anyone because of sex, age, religion, ethnic or racial background, nationality, and/or physical appearance.  GPASS has the right to deny services without discrimination to an individual.Supplies are limited, which determines the availability of these services. Clients are permitted to visit the Food Bank once per month.

n4319.label: In the Outpatient Behavioral Health Department at FPCN, our licensed clinicians provide psychotherapy for adults using individual, group, couple, and family modalities.  We work to empower people to reach their life goals and are skilled in treating trauma and loss, depression, anxiety, anger management, behavior problems, stress, attention deficit, and parenting challenges. Psychiatrists are available to those active in therapy to evaluate if medication might be helpful to a person’s treatment and to monitor its effectiveness.Services: - Mental health assessment- Individual and Couples Therapy - Medication for mental illness- Services and accommodations for people who do not speak English such as in-house staff translators or a language translation telecommunication service.   No one will be turned away for inability to pay. FPCN offers services on a sliding scale, and accepts Medicaid and Medicare. Please bring the required documents with you for your first visit.

n4320.label: Kitchen of Love provides Food Distribution to Philadelphia residents in need. Every Thursday canned foods and meat are distributed and every Friday fresh fruits and vegetables are distributed.This program provides:- Food pantryCurrently, they have a waiting list of more than 100 residents, but as they begin to expand, they will begin to pull more residents from the waiting list.

n4321.label: A partnership with Jefferson University helps Feast of Justice to offer free individual, family, or group counseling to guests to help address stress, addiction, family struggles, and more. All counselors are supervised by Pastor Tricia, but otherwise, confidentiality is of the utmost importance.  This program provides:- Individual Counseling- Family Counseling- Group CounselingAll sessions will currently be over the phone for safety during the pandemic.

n4322.label: Community Council Health Systems provides outpatient mental health services to adults and their families who are experiencing psychiatric problems. These problems include, but are not limited to psychotic conditions which are active or in remission, mood disorders, anxiety disorders and other serious and persistent mental illness. This program provides: - Psychological assessments- Psychiatric evaluation- Individual therapy- Group and family therapy- Case management - Community outreachServices are offered in person at their main office and also virtually.

n4323.label: People with intellectual disabilities and co-occurring mental illness who live in the community, or those considering a residential setting, may be eligible to receive ongoing counseling and psychiatric services through Horizon House. Treatment is individualized and includes personal and interpersonal competency skill development.Services include:- Psychological assessment- Individual, group or family counseling- Psychiatric assessment- PsychopharmacologyMedicare and Medicaid are accepted. To contact for services, please call.

n4324.label: The Bucks County Vet Center offers confidential assistance for Veterans, service members, and their families at no cost in a non-medical setting. Services include counseling for needs such as depression, posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD), and the psychological effects of military sexual trauma (MST).This program provides:- Mental health care- Connections to additional supports within the VA and your community

n4325.label: The Medical Examiner’s Office provides free bereavement support to grieving families. Bereavement support staff members can also refer families to other agencies for most medical or social service needs – including, but not limited to, burial or cremation assistance.This program provides:- Bereavement counseling over the phone- Face-to-face counseling- Information and referralsOur services are conducted mostly over the phone, and usually in a limited manner. For certain infant and child deaths, we are able to offer in-person counseling in your home.You can also get support by coming into the MEO office during regular business hours.For services or more information, please call us or visit our office.

n4326.label: In partnership with Thomas Jefferson University, Feast of Justice will begin to offer free telehealth counseling sessions as a way to provide support for stresses and anxieties associated with the coronavirus pandemic. This is a chance to speak with a counselor to help process feelings of grief, worry, and loss, as well as some physical symptoms that are associated with trauma.This program provides:- Virtual counseling If you are interested, please leave a message on the counseling help line: 215-305-9824. The program will connect you with a counselor who can meet with you over a secure phone line or on a secure video call. The counselors can provide up to three sessions free of charge to support you.

n4327.label: APM provides an array of professional behavioral health care services. Health care providers, psychiatrists, therapists, case managers, peer support specialists and other staff work together as a single team to treat the whole you, in two convenient locations in Philadelphia.Services include:- Individual Therapy- Family and Group Therapy- Psychiatric Evaluations- Psychiatric Intervention- Medication Monitoring- Couples CounselingEvening and Saturday hours are available by appointment. APM accepts Medicaid.

n4328.label: ACANA serves as a food bank and distributes food to members of the Southwest and West Philadelphia community. ACANA can also distribute clothing, toys, building materials and other resources received as donations.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional requirements - Other items as availableThis program is distributed very two weeks at ACANA.

n4329.label: The food pantry serves everyone from low income families, single parents, senior citizens, unemployed individuals, disabled veterans, working poor, and anyone else in need of our assistance. This program provides: - Food to meet basic nutritional needs

n4330.label: Using clinical protocols and activities that promote the concepts of personal strengths and resiliency, these programs combine outpatient psychiatric services with community-oriented psychiatric rehabilitation servicesMembers work on skills which will help them achieve a variety of goals ranging from psychiatric symptom reduction to independent living and vocational/educational placement. Our dedicated staff uses a blend of best practices, recovery models, and a person-centered approach that builds coping and socialization proficiency. Services are provided on-site and in a community-based setting. We also offer psychiatric resources in the form of psychiatric evaluations, medication consultation, and medication managementMembers develop the skills needed to transition from early to later adulthood. This program works with a forensic population that requires many additional skills as they attempt to move away from an institutional lifestyle.

n4331.label: The WORD program is an addictions treatment program designed to treat adolescents between the ages of 14-21, struggling with early addiction. The youth served may experience varying degrees of substance use and/or co-occurring mental health challenges.Treatment can include:- Individualized assessment- Individual or group therapy- Family therapy- Psychiatric evaluation- Medication evaluation- Care coordination- Supportive collateral servicesGroup therapy is assigned following an assessment of needs, which also assists in determining either an Intensive or outpatient level of care.Referrals to the Adolescent Addiction treatment program initiates from family and friends, as well as external agencies such as the Philadelphia Juvenile Justice system, in-patient hospitals and other human service agencies.The Consortium accepts Medicaid and offers a sliding scale fee for low-income individuals. We don't turn anyone away.

n4332.label: REBC's Food Cupboard provides a food pantry to help alleviate hunger in the community.This program provides:- Food to meet nutritional needs

n4333.label: Congregation Rodeph Shalo offers food, diapers, and feminine hygiene supplies to up to 170 families in need each Wednesday morning outside.This program provides:- Food- Diapers- Feminie supplies

n4334.label: The Crime Victim Advocacy Services of the East Division offers client centered, trauma-informed services to members of the community who have experienced a crime. This program provides:- In-person crisis counseling- Case management- Court accompaniment- Information and assistance with filing crime victim compensation claims- Legal advocacy- Linkage to resources in the community  Services are available to anyone who has experienced victimization regardless if the crime was reported to the police or not. Walk-ins are welcome.

n4335.label: Willow Grove Seventh-day Adventist Church engages in outreach each month by feeding those less fortunate out of our sister churchâ€™s site. Services include:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs

n4336.label: Case Management & Recovery Coaching Services are designed to provide individualized support to people who face mental health challenges.  Services provided in the community or in an individual’s home.This program provides:- Benefits Coordination- Coordination of Physical Health Care- Advocacy- Assistance Building and Accessing Natural Support- Housing Support and Maintenance- Crisis Prevention and Intervention- Resource Case ManagementThis service offers a combination of resource and intensive case management services. Individuals receive at least monthly face to face support from a case manager. Services can be provided more frequently if necessary. Services are provided 365 days per year, 24 hours a day by trained professional staff. An approved service plan determines the type of services needed and frequency of contacts. We accept Medicare. Please call us for more information.

n4337.label: Family First believes that the most effective way to help troubled children and adolescents is through a family-focused, home-based model designed to recognize struggles and build on family strengths. Services:- Family therapy- Individual counseling- Parent education- Case management- Family support- 24-hour on-call supportFamily First is a 32 week process. Your family is assigned a team of two Master’s level clinicians who work closely with you in the home, school and community to address not only your child’s emotional struggles but the larger systemic issues contributing to troublesome behavior. The flexibility of being in the home and community allows your therapists to address the root of your struggles and create long-term positive change.Medical Assistance (Medicaid) is accepted for these services.

n4338.label: Family Peer Support Specialists offer resources, provide advocacy, and help facilitate access to educational, behavioral health, autism, Juvenile Justice and other child welfare services in PhiladelphiaThis program provides:- One-on-one support- Resource navigation- Advocacy- Legal education, information and support

n4339.label: Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services Program provides a short term, intensive behavioral service designed to improve the lives of children and adolescents with complex psychological or behavioral needs which make it difficult to function in everyday routines.Services available:- In-home treatment- In-school treatment- Community setting treatmentCall at 267-295-8850 to schedule an appointment.

n4340.label: Mighty Writers' response to the coronavirus, Mighty Writers will bolster and expand the recently launched food-literacy distribution. Each meal box will come with writing worksheets for kids in both English and Spanish. In addition, novels and children's books will be distributed weekly.This program provides:- Food to meet nutritional needs- Books

n4341.label: The Second Antioch Baptist Church's Food Cupboard helps alleviate hunger in the community. This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needsThe pantry is available weekly on Wednesdays 12 p.m. - 2 p.m. and Saturdays 9 a.m. - 11 a.m. Organic foods are available Mondays 11 a.m. - 12 p.m.

n4342.label: The food and clothing pantry serves everyone from low income families, single parents, senior citizens, unemployed individuals, disabled veterans, working poor, and anyone else that comes to the pantry.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs- ClothingFood and clothing are distributed on Mondays and Wednesdays from 10am - 12pm.

n4343.label: BHRS is an in-home service that assists you and your family in addressing behavioral health needs by using strength-based goals and integrating community services. It is a “wraparound” service in which children and adolescents from ages three to 21 years old who have been diagnosed with mild to severe emotional/behavioral disorders and/or developmental delays are supported at home, at school, and in the community.Services include:- Behavioral health treatmentChild Guidance’s Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services are covered by the PA Medical Assistance System. If your child is diagnosed with a disability, you may qualify for Medical Assistance regardless of family income.

n4344.label: The Autism Integrated Care Program evaluates new patients for autism spectrum disorder (ASD). We also provide follow-up care to children with ASD in our Primary and Specialty Care locations.Services Available:- Mental health evaluation- Followup care- Autism Workshops

n4345.label: Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services (BHRS) is a service that focuses on the behavioral health needs of a child in his/her everyday environment. BHRS is client-centered yet family-focused, identifies the needs of the child in the home, school and community and develops a program which “wraps around” the child. Services provided include:- Weekly scheduled sessions with clinical team members in all settings relevant to their child- Clinical treatment by a well-rounded, certified team of psychologists and clinicians- A Service Specialist who can address any concerns that ariseBHRS is funded through Pennsylvania State Medical Assistance (PAMA), regardless of family income. BHRS is not exclusive, and can often be prescribed in conjunction with other behavioral health and intellectual disability services.To request our evaluation services, you can contact us by phone or fill out our on-line “intake” form. Office visits will be required to assess both the initial and ongoing needs of the child. These office visits average four times per year.

n4346.label: At Belmont Behavioral Health Hospital, we are dedicated to providing the highest quality of care to adults age 18 and older who are seeking help for mental health challenges. We offer several levels of adult inpatient programs at our facility in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.We offer:- Inpatient psychiatric care- Dual diagnosis of a substance use- Mood and personality disorder treatmentWhen you or someone you love comes to Belmont for help with a mental health crisis, our expert team will conduct a comprehensive assessment to determine which adult inpatient program is the best level of care to meet your unique needs. We provide adults with the individualized care that helps them heal and recover in the way that is most beneficial for them.Belmont Behavioral Hospital accepts Medicare & Medicaid.

n4347.label: The Center for Brief Therapy at PCOM provides a wide range of clinical assessment, treatment, and consultation services for children, adolescents and adults.This program provides:- In-person cognitive-behavioral therapy- Psychoeducational evaluations - Neuropsychological evaluations Instead of accepting insurance plans, the Center offers quality services at reasonable self-pay rates while not limiting the number of sessions. An initial 90 minute evaluation is $60 and individual 50 minute therapy sessions following the initial evaluation is $25. Individual therapy sessions with PCOM students are $10 per session.

n4348.label: North10, Philadelphia's Community Market provides a food pantry consisting of quality food items to households experiencing food insecurity. This program supplies critical nutrition to hungry individuals and families. This program provides:- Food to meet nutritional needsPlease call before visiting since registration may be required at times.

n4349.label: Camp Achieve is a uniquely designed program tailored to the special needs of youths with epilepsy. Campers can enjoy all of the fun and community building that a summer camp offers because of our trained medical staff and counselors who provide a safe environment in which to grow. Combined with epilepsy education activities, Camp Achieve is a unique place both to learn new skills and make lasting memories. There is no application fee but, if accepted, there is a registration fee of $325. Full and partial scholarships are available for eligible applicants.

n4350.label: Food with Love serves local communities in the Northeast Philadelphia area and is staffed entirely by volunteers. Food with Love/Comida conAMor operates through donations from the business community, individual sponsors, and the Revival Church congregation.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs

n4351.label: The Family Based program combines treatment and case management services geared toward children up to age 21 who are at risk for out-of-home placement due to a severe emotional and/or behavioral disorder or severe mental illness.This program provides:- Crisis intervention- Behavioral health stabilizationFamily Based uses Eco-Systemic Structural Family Therapy (ESFT), an evidence-based practice that focuses on the entire family, not just the identified child.The Consortium accepts Medicaid and offers a sliding scale fee for low-income individuals. The Consortium does not turn anyone away.

n4352.label: The Family Practice and Counseling Network (FPCN) recognizes that your emotional health is just as important as your physical health, and that there is a strong connection between mental and physical health. Recognizing the importance of attending to one’s emotional health and stability, FPCN offers Behavioral Health therapy services to help you live your life to the fullest.Services provided:- PsychotherapyWe work to empower people to reach their life goals and are skilled in treating trauma and loss, depression, anxiety, anger management, behavior problems, stress, attention deficit, and parenting challenges. Psychiatrists are available to those active in therapy to evaluate if medication might be helpful to a person’s treatment and to monitor its effectiveness.We accept Medicare and Medicaid. FPCN also offers services on a sliding scale.

n4353.label: HCCS provides outpatient services to kids who are struggling with anxiety and depression, abuse, family problems, drug and alcohol use, problems at school, and much more.Services:- Individual therapy- Skill-building and support for behavior change- Family-centered therapy and parenting strategies- Substance use assessments- Connection to resources- Medication management- Psychiatric evaluationsHispanic Community Counseling Services accepts Medicare and Medicaid.

n4354.label: GPHA provides complete diagnostic and management services for all chronic diseases. Their specialists believe that patients living with chronic disease deserve the finest healthcare available.This program provides confidential and comprehensive services including:- Confidential testing- Long-term primary medical care- Mental health counseling- The latest therapeutics and treatments- Thorough medical evaluations- Access to infectious disease specialists and a network of support They offer complete care for chronic diseases such as:- Arthritic conditions- Asthma- Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)- Diabetes- Heart disease- HIV and AIDS- Kidney disease- Viral hepatitis (hepatitis B and C)GPHA accepts Medicare and Medicaid, and offers a sliding fee scale based on income to uninsured patients. GPHA seeks to meet the needs of a diverse population, regardless of their ability to pay.

n4355.label: Jefferson Health serves adults experiencing a severe or acute mental disorder through their Adult Outpatient Psychiatry services.This program provides:- Psychosocial and psychiatric evaluations- Outpatient mental health treatmentTreatment may include:- Individual therapy- Group psychotherapy- Medical/surgical consultation The program is staffed by resident psychiatrists and psychologists and provides patients with the latest treatments for a variety of psychiatric disorders. Jefferson Health accepts Medical Assistance (Medicaid) and Self-Pay with a sliding scale fee schedule.

n4356.label: Behavioral Health Rehabilitation Services, also known as Wraparound, are services for children and adolescents who may be experiencing mental and behavioral health challenges in their daily lives. Wraparound services are a shift from traditional mental health services provided in a therapist’s office, to services where the child normally is, in the home, school, or community. This program provides:- Wraparound services - building constructive relationships and support networks- ReferralMedicaid and Medicare accepted. Private pay available.  Obtaining BHRS services first requires an evaluation by a psychologist. The request is then submitted to CBH (Community Behavioral Health) for an approval. Given the child’s diagnosis, services can be approved for 6 months – 1 year, at a time.

n4357.label: Child Guidance Resource Centers offers a supportive and effective program specifically for teenagers struggling with addiction. The Drug and Alcohol Service (D and A) is an extensive, family-based program that focuses on education, treatment and recovery from substance abuse. We supportively confront and coach our clients through the personal, academic, social and family problems created by and participating in the use of drugs and alcohol.Our treatment includes- Individual therapy- Family therapy- Group therapy- Relapse prevention techniques - After-care planningChild Guidance Resource Centers accepts Medicaid for their services.

n4358.label: The Food Cupboard provides quality food items to households experiencing food insecurity. This program supplies critical nutrition to hungry individuals and families. This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs

n4359.label: *Please check dvch.org for COVID-19 updates or call (215) 222-2466*As of Monday, March 23, 2020, we will continue to see all scheduled patients for visits by phone (telemedicine)In partnership with Delaware Valley Community Health and Fairmount Primary Care Health Center, the coordination of medical and behavioral health care is provided for adults with mental health needs. Its focus is to support individuals live longer, healthier, and more satisfying lives in the community. Coordination and collaboration of these services have been proven to reduce the barriers to recoveryServices offered:- Primary care- Behavioral health careWe accept Medicare and Medicaid and also offer a sliding scale fee. For more information, please call us.

n4360.label: Seeds of Potential offers creative and holistic counseling and coaching services alongside verbal counseling to provide a rich opportunity to meet individual and family needs.Services include:- Individual counseling- Family counseling- Group counseling - Mindfulness workshops- Dance/Movement Therapy- Creative Art Therapy- Play therapy- Sand Tray, sandbox or sand play therapy- Attachment TherapyEach therapist has a sliding scale to make therapy more affordable and available. Please contact us to discuss sliding scale options.

n4361.label: The BBVS provides assistance Pennsylvanians who are blind or visually impaired gain the skills necessary to live and work independently in their communities.Services offered:- Employment support- Information and referral- Independent living skills- Entrepreneurial skills and support Most services are free and some are income-based. Please call us for more information.

n4362.label: ACHIEVEability's Family Self-Sufficiency Program (FSSP) offers affordable housing and wrap around coaching supports to families experiencing housing instability. Through FSSP, professional coaches with a background in social work will provide clients with one-on-one counseling and guidance on career, education, financial, parenting, and personal goals. These supports add structure while parents go back to school for a better degree, navigate the start of a career,  establish next steps in housing, and step into a homebuying pipeline to secure generational wealth for their family.

n4363.label: Puentes de Salud offers all diabetic patients individual counseling with a nurse educator that covers the principles of sugar monitoring at home, dietary modification, physical activity and weight managementServices:-  Individual counseling for diabetes managementYou can make an appointment by visiting us during the first hour of clinic hours each day: monday 12:30-1:30, weds 12:30-1:30, and thurs 10-11am.

n4364.label: Child and Family Outpatient Services provide mental health evaluations and treatment to children and adolescents ages 3-18 and their families, who are residents of Philadelphia.Services available:- Mental health evaluation- Behavioral health treatmentReferrals are received from area public, parochial and private schools, social service agencies, pediatricians, health centers, managed care organizations (primarily Community Behavioral Health), police, courts, and residents.Hall-Mercer Community Behavioral Health Center offers Mental Health services for those clients who are eligible for Medical Assistance in Philadelphia County. Fee for Service Medicare is also accepted for those who live in Hall Mercer's catchment area. Self-pay on a sliding-scale is also an option for receiving services.

n4365.label: Wedge Recovery Center offers the Early Intervention Program for Philadelphia residents between the ages 12-21 who are currently using substances or who are otherwise at risk for substance abuse because of factors in the environmental, school, legal, family, mental health, peer & social, and dependency system domains.We offer:- Substance Use Screening and Assessment- Evidenced-based Substance Abuse Education- Intervention Counseling- Family Counseling- Community/School/Child service outreach- Individualized Service Planning- Group Psychoeducational Sessions- Parental Education/Consultation- Service Linkage

n4366.label: Annex West Health Clinic offers many other services besides primary care. Adults and children have a single point of access for care that addresses all aspects of the person and family.Services provided:- Case management- Outreach and Health Insurance Enrollment- Medication Assisted Treatment- Rapid walk-in HIV testing- Mind/Body Practices (on a monthly basis)We accept Medicare, Medicaid, and most major insurances. For those without insurance, we offer services on a sliding scale.

n4367.label: Church World Service Inc offers a network of respite shelters to assist those awaiting a court date with the US government after going through US custody. This program provides:- Short-term shelter- Meals- Clothing- Hygiene products- Access to telephones to call loved ones- Connection to resettlement offices, member congregations, sanctuary churches and other service providers that can assist with housing, legal services, support services and resource sharingCWS has refugee and immigration offices spread across different states. Many of these offices are managed through partnerships with local organizations, while some are directly overseen by CWS and as such set the standard for resettlement in local communities.

n4368.label: Developmental behavioral pediatrics is a specialty that focuses on children’s growth and development. Nemours Children's Health System works together with a team of medical specialists to develop individualized treatment plans for kids with developmental delays, developmental disabilities or medical conditions that affect their learning and behavior.This program provides: - A comprehensive evaluation- A physical exam, checking things like coordination, reflexes and muscle tone- Observations of how your child communicates, moves and plays- Further testing, if needed, to evaluate concerns such as speech and language abilities, fine motor skills, gross motor skillsNemours Children's Health System may also provide a full report of its findings and, if a problem is diagnosed, specific recommendations (such as at-home or school-based therapies, early intervention services, counseling and/or medications, guidance and referrals about agencies and educational programs that may be helpful).Nemours Children's Health System primarily focuses on children under the age of 7 years old in this specialty. They have a limited number of appointments for older children and teens. They prioritize appointments for young children, but please know that there can be a significant wait — potentially six months or more — for appointments for children of any age.Nemours Children's Health System accepts Medicaid and Medicare, and offers financial assistance to eligible uninsured families. Please call 800-416-4441 if you are in the Delaware/Greater Philadelphia area and call 407-650-7715 if you are in Central Florida.

n4369.label: Rebecca Asch's Counseling Services help people learn how to deal more effectively with situations in their lives such as depression, addiction and substance abuse, stress, problems with self- esteem, grief, issues related to mental and emotional health, and relational problems.This program provides:- Art Therapy- Individual and Couples Counseling- Group TherapyThis provider offers a sliding scale based off of availability of slots. Please use the contact page of their website for more information.

n4370.label: The Center for the Treatment and Study of Anxiety at the University of Pennsylvania specializes in cognitive-behavioral therapy treatment for children and adults who suffer from anxiety and traumatic stress disorders.Services provided:- Cognitive behavioral therapy We treat the following disorders: - Obsessive-compulsive- Post-traumatic stress- Social anxiety- Generalized anxiety disorder- Panic disorder- Trichotillomania and excoriation- Specific phobiaBefore entering a treatment program at the Center, patients first complete a phone screening interview with one of our intake coordinators. If appropriate, patients then schedule and complete an extensive psychological evaluation with a clinician.Assessment and treatment is often free or reduced cost if you are eligible to participate in any of our treatment research programs. If you are uninsured or underinsured, low cost treatment options are available with our advanced doctoral student clinicians.

n4371.label: When disaster strikes, The Salvation Army is oftenAMong the first on the scene. In disaster relief operation, The Salvation Army's first aim is to meet the basic needs of those affected _ both survivors and first responders.This program provides:- Food- Disaster shelter- Financial assistance- Spiritual care- Emotional carePlease call this provider for more information.

n4372.label: Our Adult Residential Rehabilitation program is an 8 and a half month non-clinical residential program in which adults struggling to live independent and healthy lives can conquer their demons and gain the skills necessary to improve themselves and live full lives. We help individuals living with additions, depression or other difficulties become independent, contributory members of society.This program provides:- Short-term housingResidents in the program receive: - Mentoring- Peer, group and spiritual support- Daily life skills training- Non-clinical drug and mental health treatmentThere is no cost for this program. However, if a resident does have an income (social security, pension, etc.), they will be required to pay $20/day to help cover program costs.Interested persons should call to schedule an interview.

n4373.label: Neighbor to Neighbor's Food Cupboard, a food program, for Delaware County residents, delivers urgently needed food to low-income community residents. Each food package provides breakfast, lunch and dinner.This program provides:- Fresh vegetables & fruits- Dairy items- Eggs- Canned goods- Baked goods & pastries- Cereal- Poultry, fish, & meatWhen available, infant foods dietary supplements are distributed.Eligible households receive food packages one Thursday per month. Food packages may also be distributed on an emergency or one-time basis for any community household in need.In response to the COVID-19 pandemic, Neighbor to Neighbor Community Development Corporation, Inc. is no longer permitting non-essential staff inside of our building. When you arrive to utilize the food pantry. Please remain outside and a staff member will come to you.

n4374.label: The Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP) provides USDA-donated food to low-income seniors through monthly CSFP/senior boxes. Philabundance packs and distributes these boxes to strategically chosen locations throughout the Delaware Valley.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needs Boxes contain approximately 30 pounds of canned and boxed food including vegetables, fruit, juice, pasta, milk, cereal, canned meat and a non-meat protein.

n4375.label: Each week we supply 50 - 60 households with packages that include fresh and non-perishable foods. Using prescribed products and quantities, you are invited into the cupboard to choose the items you need. Packages supply food for 3 days and may be received once within a 30- day period.  This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needsWhen available, personal hygiene items, baby products, and household cleaning supplies are provided for you.

n4376.label: The Bureau of Blindness and Visual Services for Children provides assistance to children with visual impairments to ensure they reach their maximum educational and vocational potential. Services Provided:- Advocacy for educational services- Guidance and counseling for children and their families- Community orientation and mobility (travel) instruction- Children's summer programs- Adaptive equipmentMost programs are free while some are income-based. Please contact us via phone for more information.

n4377.label: CREATE is a year round, innovative group outpatient service for children and young adults with a diagnosis of autism. Participants will build social and communication skills, improve problem solving and emotional regulation, and enhance flexibility and motivation. Each day in CREATE, every child will participate in evidence-based interactive therapies including:- ABA (Applied Behavior Analysis)- PRT (Pivotal Response Therapy)- Hidden Curriculum- QuestTM- Social ThinkingTM- social stories- behavior mappingFamily therapy and parent workshops are also a component of CREATE. CREATE is an outpatient service, so anyone can make refer someone. Parents, teachers, pediatricians, grandparents, clinicians, case managers, anyone can make a referral, even clients themselves!CREATE is covered by Medical Assistance and most private insurance plans. In most cases, transportation is provided.

n4378.label: Behavioral Health Services program offers depression counseling and management, behavioral health counseling and management and alcohol dependency management. Services include:- Behavioral HealthPayment methods include private insurance, medicaid/medicare, and sliding scale fees for uninsured clients.

n4379.label: The Bilingual Domestic Violence Program collaborates with other domestic violence programs and the City of Philadelphia to operate the Philadelphia Domestic Violence Hotline which provides the following for victims of domestic and dating abuse:Services include:- Crisis intervention- Counseling- Safety planning- Referrals for domestic abuse services and other needsThe hotline is toll free and is answered by trained counselors 24-hours a day and offers services in multiple languages. The hotline is also covered by local agencies Congreso de Latinos, Women in Transition, and Women Against Abuse.

n4380.label: Hall-Mercer Community Mental Health Center offers a variety of adult behavioral health services to meet the needs of the community.This program provides:- Intake & Continuity of Care Cervices- Blended Case Management- ACCESS Intensive Case Management- Prevention & Recovery Services (PARS-RC)- Homeless Outreach Services

n4381.label: The food pantry provides quality food items to households experiencing food insecurity for individuals and families in Fox Chase and surrounding neighborhoods. This program supplies critical nutrition to hungry individuals and families. This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional needsThis program operates on the Second and Fourth Saturdays of each month.

n4382.label: Philadelphia Children’s Crisis Response Center provides the on-site Crisis Stabilization Unit (CSU) as an option for children and young adults experiencing extreme distress who can benefit from an intensive, short-term, coordinated treatment experience. This program provides:- Mental health assessmentDepending upon the results of this assessment, we may recommend the following services:- Therapeutic stabilization- Admission into our on-site Crisis Stabilization Unit (CSU) - Referral to appropriate level of care - Crisis/aftercare planning Typical length of stay is three to five days. If you are members of Community Behavioral Health (Philadelphia’s Medicaid behavioral health program), then treatment is provided at no cost to the patient.

n4383.label: COMHAR offers outpatient evaluation and counseling for youth beginning at age 5. Services are all focused on building familial strength and community support.  Services are available both for children with existing behavioral health diagnoses and for those whose families want them to be assessedThis program provides:- Psychiatric evaluation- Medication management- Family therapy- Individual and group therapyCOMHAR accepts Medicaid.

n4384.label: Springfield Psychological's child & adolescent services strive to achieve long-term improvement for children with emotional challenges, ADHD, or learning disabilities with a multi-level approach.Services include:- Psychotherapy- Parent, teacher, and pediatrician consulting- Coping skills developmentWe accept most insurance plans including Medicare (but not if Medicaid is your secondary).Springfield Psychological is now providing Telehealth Services for both outpatient therapy and medication management to support new and existing patients from the comfort of your own home during the Coronavirus outbreak. Telehealth services are now covered by ALL commercial insurance and Medicare.

n4385.label: The Caring Together program helps women and their children overcome substance abuse and start a new life without drugs and alcohol. The program utilizes a multiphase, multidisciplinary approach to treat the addiction and any other issues that may have caused or resulted from substance use.This program provides:- Comprehensive treatment needs assessment- Individual and group counseling- Psychiatric evaluation and treatment, including medication-assisted treatment (MAT)- Substance use education including relapse prevention- Trauma-informed care- Anger management classes- Life skills training- Grief and loss groups- DHS evaluations- A safe recovery environment- Access to medical and dental care- Transportation assistance- GED/ABE program referrals- Medical insurance assistance- Employment training referrals- Housing referralsFor women with children, the program offers:- Parenting education- Family reunification assistance- Coordinated case management- Child care during treatment- Child development assessments- Assistance with health care, head-start programs, after-school programs, school-related issues, etc.The program offers a safe recovery environment and is especially beneficial to those who are pregnant or parenting, members of the LGBTQI+ community, and survivors of abuse, as well as anyone experiencing mental health challenges or domestic violence.

n4386.label: Kingdom Life Christian Center provides food assistance to anyone in the community. We also provide diapers and formula, available while supplies last.This program provides:- Food to meet basic nutritional requirements Check website to see when the food distribution is offered as the times and dates are subject to change.

n4387.label: The Child and Adolescent Anxiety Disorders Clinic provides help for children who suffer from anxiety disorders. The clinic uses a cognitive behavioral therapy approach to treat anxiety and their standard treatment is 16 weeks, although the exact length varies based on the client’s needs.This program provides:- Cognitive behavioral therapyThe fee for therapy sessions for this clinic is determined based on a sliding scale depending on estimated annual household income and number of dependents.

n4388.label: The Better Living Center is dedicated to providing resources and information to empower the residents of Chester and Delaware County to improve their lives.This program provides:- An Emergency Food Pantry- Free Computer and Internet Access- After-School Programs- YMCA Summer Camp in partnership with the Rocky Run YMCA- Job Training, Career Development and Employment Searches- Healthy Lifestyle, Healthcare and Mental Health Information

n4389.label: The Fairmount Primary Care Center at Girard Medical Center (FPCC-GMC) brings comprehensive primary care medical services to the patients of Girard Medical Center, furthering our commitment to making healthcare accessible to patients of all needs.This program provides:- Adult Medicine- Gynecology- HIV/HEP C Care- Medication Assisted TreatmentA sliding fee scale is available to eligible patients.

n4390.label: Addiction Medicine and Health Advocates, Inc. (AMHA) provides evidence-based substance abuse treatment, which is continuously responsive to the needs of the patients and their families.AMHA is an outpatient facility consisting of two levels of care: Outpatient and Intensive Outpatient. Methadone Maintenance is provided on both levels of care.Services provided include: Assessment and intake Medication-assisted treatment (methadone maintenance) Individual counseling & psychotherapy Group psychotherapy Psycho education Medical & psychiatric evaluations  Aftercare & follow up services  Referrals (educational, medical, vocational, psychiatric, higher levels of care) Couples & family therapy Drug screening To begin the admissions process, please contact us. You may also come in person between the hours of 9AM and 4PM, Monday through Friday. You will be asked to complete a short screening interview and if initial eligibility criteria is met, you will be scheduled for an assessment.At the time of your assessment, you will need to bring: Current, state-issued photo ID Insurance cardAMHA is a private not-for-profit organization dependent upon patient fees for its continuance. Most of our patients have some form of insurance or medical assistance that pays for medication-assisted treatment services. Individuals who do not have insurance are responsible for payment or they may be eligible for county funding.
